HMS-Mirror also generates distcp plans alongside the schema migration to facilitate this external data copy.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Reads table definition from LEFT (source) -- The full CREATE TABLE definition, including columns, SerDe, storage format, LOCATION, and TBLPROPERTIES, is retrieved from the source cluster's Hive Metastore.

  2. Translates storage locations from source namespace to target namespace -- The TranslatorService replaces the source HDFS/S3/ABFS namespace with the target namespace. Translation uses ordered location maps (auto-generated from warehouse configuration and/or user-provided custom mappings), with longest-path-match-wins semantics.

  3. Converts table types if needed (managed non-ACID to EXTERNAL + PURGE on Hive 3 upgrade) -- When migrating from a legacy Hive (1/2) environment to a non-legacy (Hive 3+) environment and convertManaged() returns true, managed non-transactional tables are converted to EXTERNAL TABLE with EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE=true, preserving data ownership semantics.

  4. Strips ACID properties if migrating transactional tables with downgrade -- When migrateACID.downgrade=true, transactional properties (transactional, transactional_properties, BUCKETING_VERSION) are removed, the table is made EXTERNAL with PURGE, and the DOWNGRADED_FROM_ACID property is set. The LOCATION is also stripped because Hive 3 manages ACID table locations internally.

  5. Generates CREATE TABLE DDL for RIGHT (target) -- The buildOutSql() method produces USE <database> and CREATE TABLE (or CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE) statements targeting the RIGHT cluster, incorporating all translated locations, converted types, and adjusted properties.

  6. Generates partition discovery statements -- If partition metadata was loaded (loadMetadataDetails=true), explicit ALTER TABLE ADD PARTITION ... LOCATION statements are generated with individually translated partition paths. Otherwise, if the RIGHT cluster supports auto-discovery via MSCK, a MSCK REPAIR TABLE statement is generated.

  7. Optionally generates ALTER TABLE SET OWNER -- When ownershipTransfer.table=true and the target cluster is non-legacy, an ALTER TABLE SET OWNER statement is generated to transfer the table owner from the source.

docs.title: Table LOCATION
docs.text:
The source namespace is replaced with the target namespace. The translation mode determines how the path is constructed:

  • RELATIVE -- Simple namespace swap. The path suffix after the namespace is preserved as-is. 

    hdfs://hdp-source/apps/hive/warehouse/sales.db/orders
-->  s3a://data-lake-bucket/apps/hive/warehouse/sales.db/orders

  • ALIGNED -- Warehouse plans drive the target path structure, potentially reorganizing the directory layout. 

    hdfs://hdp-source/apps/hive/warehouse/sales.db/orders
-->  s3a://data-lake-bucket/warehouse/external/sales.db/orders

docs.title: Partition Locations
docs.text: Each partition path is individually translated through TranslatorService.translatePartitionLocations(). This is critical when partitions have non-standard locations that differ from the table's base path.

docs.title: Managed to External Conversion
docs.text:
On Hive 2 to Hive 3 upgrade (legacy to non-legacy), non-ACID managed tables become EXTERNAL with EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE=true:

Hive 2 (Legacy)                    Hive 3 (CDP)
+-------------------+              +-----------------------------+
| MANAGED TABLE     |   -------->  | EXTERNAL TABLE              |
| (non-ACID)        |              | + EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE=true |
+-------------------+              +-----------------------------+

Additional properties set during conversion: - hmsMirror_LegacyManaged=true -- marks that this table was originally a legacy managed table - hmsMirror_Converted=true -- marks that the table type was converted

docs.title: ACID Handling
docs.text:
When migrateACID.on=true and migrateACID.downgrade=true:

  • transactional property removed
  • transactional_properties property removed
  • BUCKETING_VERSION property removed
  • Table made EXTERNAL
  • EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE=true set (unless noPurge=true)
  • DOWNGRADED_FROM_ACID=true set
  • LOCATION stripped (Hive 3 manages ACID locations internally; the downgraded table uses the database default external location)
  • Artificial buckets removed if bucket count is at or below artificialBucketThreshold

docs.title: Properties Stripped
docs.text:
The following stale or irrelevant properties are removed from the target definition:

  • COLUMN_STATS_ACCURATE
  • numFiles
  • numRows
  • rawDataSize
  • totalSize
  • discover.partitions (removed then re-added based on target cluster configuration)
  • transient_lastDdlTime
  • external (controlled by CREATE statement, not TBLPROPERTIES)
  • last_modified_by
  • last_modified_time

docs.title: Properties Added
docs.text:
• hmsMirror_Metadata_Stage1 -- timestamp of when the schema migration was performed
  • discover.partitions=true -- set for external/converted partitioned tables when the target cluster has auto partition discovery enabled
  • EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE=true -- set when the target should own the data (upgrade conversion, or explicit ownership)

docs.title: Iceberg Conversion
docs.text: When icebergConversion.enable=true, the target table definition is converted to Iceberg format: - Non-Iceberg storage constructs are stripped from the definition - format-version property is set to the configured Iceberg version (1 or 2)

docs.title: Data Mapping
docs.text:
• NO data movement -- This strategy only handles metadata (schema definitions and metastore entries).
  • Assumes data is moved externally and arrives at the translated location paths before queries are run against the target tables.
  • The generated distcp plans (always produced by SCHEMA_ONLY) provide a mapping of source paths to target paths that should be used for the external data copy.
  • The generated locations must match where the external data copy places the files. If the data lands at a different path than the translated LOCATION, the table will not resolve its data.

docs.title: Configuration Parameters That Affect This Strategy
docs.text: Parameter Effect transfer.warehouse.externalDirectory Target warehouse base path for external tables. Drives location translation when using ALIGNED mode. transfer.warehouse.managedDirectory Target warehouse base path for managed tables. Drives location translation for ACID tables (before downgrade strips LOCATION). transfer.targetNamespace Target storage namespace (e.g., hdfs://new-cluster or s3a://bucket). transfer.storageMigration.translationType RELATIVE (simple namespace swap) or ALIGNED (warehouse plan-driven layout). migrateACID.on Include ACID tables in the migration. Without this, ACID tables are skipped. migrateACID.downgrade Convert ACID tables to EXTERNAL+PURGE by stripping transactional properties. migrateACID.artificialBucketThreshold Remove CLUSTERED BY ... INTO n BUCKETS if n is at or below this value (default: 2). Cleans up legacy artificial bucketing. migrateACID.partitionLimit Skip ACID tables with more partitions than this limit (default: 500). migrateVIEW.on Include views in migration. Views always use SCHEMA_ONLY logic regardless of the top-level strategy. migrateNonNative Include non-native tables (HBase, Kafka, JDBC). These are passed through as-is with a warning about dependency availability. createIfNotExists Adds IF NOT EXISTS to CREATE TABLE DDL. When true and the table already exists, creation will fail softly and continue with partition statements. forceExternalLocation Always include the LOCATION clause in CREATE TABLE, even when the default database location would suffice. enableAutoTableStats / enableAutoColumnStats Generates ANALYZE TABLE statements after table creation to compute statistics on the target. copyAvroSchemaUrls Preserves the avro.schema.url table property and copies the referenced Avro schema file between clusters. Requires clusters to be linked. icebergConversion.enable Convert the target table to Iceberg format during schema creation. icebergConversion.version Iceberg format version (1 or 2). Default: 2. icebergConversion.fileTypeTranslation Target file format for Iceberg conversion (PARQUET, ORC, AVRO, STANDARD). ownershipTransfer.table Generate ALTER TABLE SET OWNER to transfer table ownership from source to target. readOnly Create tables without PURGE flag, suitable for DR or testing scenarios. noPurge Force removal of PURGE flag from all tables. sync Enable synchronization mode: drop and recreate tables if schemas differ, drop tables on RIGHT that no longer exist on LEFT.

docs.title: Where This Strategy Is NOT Applicable
docs.text:
• When you need data to move as part of the migration process. Use SQL, EXPORT_IMPORT, or HYBRID instead -- these strategies generate INSERT...SELECT or EXPORT/IMPORT commands that move data through the Hive engine.
  • When you want RIGHT to query LEFT's data directly without copying it. Use LINKED instead -- it preserves source locations so both clusters read the same physical files.
  • When migrating within the same cluster to a new storage location. Use STORAGE_MIGRATION instead -- it handles in-place data movement with table renaming and archiving.
  • When both clusters share the same physical storage. Use COMMON instead -- it creates schemas with the same locations, no translation needed.

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
1. Pre-migration validation -- Create schemas on the target cluster, validate that they compile and are structurally correct, then run a separate data copy (distcp or cloud replication) as a second phase. The dry-run mode (execute=false) makes this completely non-destructive.

  2. HDP to CDP schema upgrade -- Migrate table definitions from an HDP 2.x cluster (legacy Hive) to a CDP cluster (Hive 3). Managed non-ACID tables are automatically converted to EXTERNAL+PURGE. ACID tables can be downgraded. Run distcp separately to copy data from HDFS source to the translated target paths.

  3. Disaster recovery -- Replicate schemas to a standby cluster. Data is replicated independently via HDFS snapshots, S3 cross-region replication, or similar mechanisms. Use readOnly=true to avoid setting PURGE flags on the DR cluster, preventing accidental data deletion.

  4. Cloud migration with distcp -- Create target schemas pointing to S3/ABFS/GCS locations, then run distcp from on-premises HDFS to the matching cloud paths. The generated distcp plans provide the exact source-to-target path mapping.

  5. Schema synchronization -- Use sync=true to keep two clusters' metastores aligned. Tables that exist on LEFT but not RIGHT are created. Tables that exist on RIGHT but not LEFT are dropped. Tables that exist on both but differ are replaced. Tables that match are left alone (or have partitions amended).

docs.title: (a) Simple External Table -- HDP to CDP
docs.text:
Source (LEFT, legacy Hive on hdfs://HDP50): 

-- Original table on source cluster (legacy managed, non-ACID)
CREATE TABLE `sales.db`.`customers`(
  `c_id` bigint,
  `c_name` varchar(100),
  `c_email` varchar(200),
  `c_signup_date` date)
ROW FORMAT SERDE
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcSerde'
STORED AS INPUTFORMAT
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcInputFormat'
OUTPUTFORMAT
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcOutputFormat'
LOCATION
  'hdfs://HDP50/apps/hive/warehouse/sales.db/customers';

Generated RIGHT SQL (target CDP on hdfs://CDP-PROD): 

USE sales;

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE `customers`(
  `c_id` bigint,
  `c_name` varchar(100),
  `c_email` varchar(200),
  `c_signup_date` date)
ROW FORMAT SERDE
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcSerde'
STORED AS INPUTFORMAT
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcInputFormat'
OUTPUTFORMAT
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcOutputFormat'
LOCATION
  'hdfs://CDP-PROD/warehouse/external/sales.db/customers'
TBLPROPERTIES (
  'hmsMirror_Metadata_Stage1'='2026-02-16 10:30:00',
  'hmsMirror_LegacyManaged'='true',
  'hmsMirror_Converted'='true',
  'external.table.purge'='true',
  'discover.partitions'='true');

docs.title: (b) Partitioned Table with Metadata Loaded
docs.text:
Source (LEFT): partitioned external table with explicit partition locations.

Generated RIGHT SQL: 

USE sales;

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE `orders`(
  `o_id` bigint,
  `o_customer_id` bigint,
  `o_total` decimal(12,2),
  `o_status` varchar(20))
PARTITIONED BY (
  `o_order_date` string)
ROW FORMAT SERDE
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcSerde'
STORED AS INPUTFORMAT
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcInputFormat'
OUTPUTFORMAT
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcOutputFormat'
LOCATION
  'hdfs://CDP-PROD/warehouse/external/sales.db/orders'
TBLPROPERTIES (
  'hmsMirror_Metadata_Stage1'='2026-02-16 10:30:00',
  'hmsMirror_Converted'='true',
  'external.table.purge'='true',
  'discover.partitions'='true');

ALTER TABLE orders ADD
  PARTITION (o_order_date='2025-01') LOCATION 'hdfs://CDP-PROD/warehouse/external/sales.db/orders/o_order_date=2025-01'
  PARTITION (o_order_date='2025-02') LOCATION 'hdfs://CDP-PROD/warehouse/external/sales.db/orders/o_order_date=2025-02'
  PARTITION (o_order_date='2025-03') LOCATION 'hdfs://CDP-PROD/warehouse/external/sales.db/orders/o_order_date=2025-03';

When metadata is NOT loaded, the partition statements are replaced with: 

MSCK REPAIR TABLE sales.orders;

docs.title: (c) ACID Downgrade
docs.text:
Source (LEFT): a transactional (ACID) managed table on legacy Hive, bucketed into 2 buckets.

-- Original on source
CREATE TABLE `sales.db`.`transactions`(
  `txn_id` bigint,
  `txn_amount` decimal(12,2),
  `txn_type` varchar(20))
CLUSTERED BY (txn_id) INTO 2 BUCKETS
ROW FORMAT SERDE
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcSerde'
STORED AS INPUTFORMAT
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcInputFormat'
OUTPUTFORMAT
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcOutputFormat'
TBLPROPERTIES (
  'transactional'='true',
  'transactional_properties'='default');

Generated RIGHT SQL (with migrateACID.on=true, migrateACID.downgrade=true, artificialBucketThreshold=2): 

USE sales;

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE `transactions`(
  `txn_id` bigint,
  `txn_amount` decimal(12,2),
  `txn_type` varchar(20))
ROW FORMAT SERDE
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcSerde'
STORED AS INPUTFORMAT
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcInputFormat'
OUTPUTFORMAT
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcOutputFormat'
TBLPROPERTIES (
  'hmsMirror_Metadata_Stage1'='2026-02-16 10:30:00',
  'external.table.purge'='true',
  'DOWNGRADED_FROM_ACID'='true',
  'discover.partitions'='true');

Note: LOCATION is stripped (Hive 3 will place the data at the database's default external location). The CLUSTERED BY clause is removed because the bucket count (2) is at or below the artificialBucketThreshold. Transactional properties are removed.

docs.title: (d) Iceberg Conversion
docs.text:
Source (LEFT): standard ORC external table.

Generated RIGHT SQL (with icebergConversion.enable=true, icebergConversion.version=2): 

USE analytics;

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE `events`(
  `event_id` bigint,
  `event_type` varchar(50),
  `event_payload` string,
  `event_ts` timestamp)
STORED BY 'org.apache.iceberg.mr.hive.HiveIcebergStorageHandler'
LOCATION
  's3a://data-lake-bucket/warehouse/external/analytics.db/events'
TBLPROPERTIES (
  'hmsMirror_Metadata_Stage1'='2026-02-16 10:30:00',
  'format-version'='2',
  'external.table.purge'='true',
  'discover.partitions'='true');

Note: The original ROW FORMAT SERDE and STORED AS clauses are replaced by the Iceberg storage handler. The format-version property is set according to the configured Iceberg version.

docs.title: Implementation Reference
docs.text: Component Path Strategy class server/src/main/java/.../datastrategy/SchemaOnlyDataStrategy.java Base class (buildTableSchema) server/src/main/java/.../datastrategy/DataStrategyBase.java CopySpec (migration directives) server/src/main/java/.../CopySpec.java Translator service server/src/main/java/.../service/TranslatorService.java Table utilities common/src/main/java/.../util/TableUtils.java Strategy enum common/src/main/java/.../domain/DataStrategyEnum.java

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Data Strategy Concept
  • LINKED Strategy

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Creates external tables on the target cluster that point directly to the source cluster's data locations. No namespace translation is performed -- the original storage paths are preserved exactly so that both clusters access the same physical data files. This is a non-destructive way to give the target cluster read access to source data without any data movement or duplication.

LINKED is primarily intended for testing and validation scenarios: it lets you stand up a new cluster (e.g., CDP) and immediately run queries against production data that lives on the original cluster (e.g., HDP), verifying Hive compatibility before committing to a full migration.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Reads table definition from LEFT (source) -- The full CREATE TABLE definition is retrieved from the source cluster's Hive Metastore, including all columns, SerDe, storage format, LOCATION, and TBLPROPERTIES.

  2. Creates EXTERNAL TABLE on RIGHT using LEFT's exact storage locations (no translation) -- The CopySpec is configured with replaceLocation=false, meaning the source LOCATION is preserved as-is. The table on RIGHT will resolve data from the same HDFS/S3/ABFS paths that LEFT uses.

  3. Does NOT set EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE -- The CopySpec is configured with takeOwnership=false. Since RIGHT does not own the data, the PURGE flag is never set. This prevents accidental data deletion if the table is dropped on the target cluster.

  4. Generates partition discovery -- After the CREATE TABLE, either explicit ALTER TABLE ADD PARTITION ... LOCATION statements (when partition metadata is loaded) or MSCK REPAIR TABLE (for auto-discovery) is generated. Partition locations are also preserved from the source without translation.

  5. Reuses SchemaOnlyDataStrategy for SQL generation -- The buildOutSql() method delegates to SchemaOnlyDataStrategy.buildOutSql(), which handles the USE, CREATE, DROP, REPLACE, and partition SQL generation. The key difference is in the definition phase: LINKED does not translate locations.

docs.title: Locations Are NOT Translated
docs.text:
This is the defining characteristic of the LINKED strategy. Source paths are preserved exactly:

Source:  hdfs://HDP50/apps/hive/warehouse/sales.db/orders
Target:  hdfs://HDP50/apps/hive/warehouse/sales.db/orders  (same path)

The RIGHT cluster must have network-level access to the LEFT cluster's storage for this to work. This typically means: - HDFS federation or cross-cluster HDFS access via dfs.nameservices - Cross-account S3 access via IAM role policies or bucket policies - Shared NAS/SAN storage visible to both clusters

docs.title: Table Type Forced to EXTERNAL
docs.text: Regardless of the source table type, the target table is created as EXTERNAL. This is because LINKED tables are read-only references -- they should never modify the underlying data.

docs.title: EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE Is NOT Set
docs.text: Since the data is owned by the source cluster, the PURGE flag is explicitly excluded. This prevents a DROP TABLE on the RIGHT cluster from deleting data on the LEFT cluster.

docs.title: ACID Tables Are NOT Supported
docs.text:
The LinkedDataStrategy.build() method explicitly checks for ACID tables and rejects them:

if (TableUtils.isACID(let)) {
    tableMirror.addIssue(Environment.LEFT, "You can't 'LINK' ACID tables.");
    rtn = Boolean.FALSE;
}

ACID (transactional) tables use internal delta file structures managed by the Hive metastore. These files cannot be read as a simple external table because the ACID reader requires metastore transaction state that is not shared between clusters.

docs.title: Views Are NOT Supported
docs.text: Views reference other tables by name. If the underlying tables do not exist on the RIGHT cluster (or exist under different database names), the view will fail. LINKED does not attempt to resolve or rewrite view SQL.

docs.title: Non-Native Tables Are NOT Supported
docs.text: Tables backed by external systems (HBase, Kafka, JDBC) require those systems to be configured and accessible from the RIGHT cluster. LINKED does not validate or configure these dependencies.

docs.title: Statistics Properties Stripped
docs.text: The same stale properties removed by all strategies are stripped: - COLUMN_STATS_ACCURATE - numFiles, numRows, rawDataSize, totalSize - discover.partitions (removed then re-added based on target cluster config) - transient_lastDdlTime - external, last_modified_by, last_modified_time

docs.title: HMS_MIRROR_METADATA_FLAG Added
docs.text: The hmsMirror_Metadata_Stage1 property is added with the migration timestamp, marking the table as having been created by HMS-Mirror.

docs.title: Managed to External Conversion
docs.text: When migrating from a legacy (Hive 2) source, managed non-ACID tables are converted to EXTERNAL (via copySpec.setUpgrade(true) when convertManaged() returns true). However, unlike SCHEMA_ONLY, the PURGE flag is NOT set because the RIGHT cluster does not own the data.

docs.title: Data Mapping
docs.text:
• NO data movement -- Both clusters read the same physical files at the same storage paths.
  • Requires network connectivity -- The RIGHT cluster must be able to access LEFT's storage. This means HDFS cross-cluster access, HDFS federation, shared S3 bucket access, or shared NAS storage.
  • Changes to data on LEFT are immediately visible from RIGHT -- Since both clusters point to the same files, any data modifications (inserts, updates, compactions) on the source are immediately reflected in queries on the target.
  • Write conflicts are possible -- If both clusters attempt to write to the same locations, data corruption can occur. LINKED tables should be treated as read-only on the target.

docs.title: Configuration Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Effect createIfNotExists Adds IF NOT EXISTS to CREATE TABLE DDL. enableAutoTableStats Generates ANALYZE TABLE after table creation. copyAvroSchemaUrls Preserves avro.schema.url and copies Avro schema files. ownershipTransfer.table Generates ALTER TABLE SET OWNER for ownership transfer. sync Enables synchronization mode: creates missing tables, drops orphaned tables, replaces tables with schema differences. readOnly Should be set to true for LINKED. Creates tables without PURGE flag (redundant for LINKED since PURGE is never set, but reinforces the safety intent). noPurge Should be set to true for LINKED. Forces no PURGE flag. skipLinkCheck Must be false for LINKED. Clusters MUST be linked (storage-accessible) for this strategy to work. dbPrefix Prepend a string to database names on the RIGHT cluster (useful for testing to avoid conflicts). dbRename Rename a single database on the target.

docs.title: Parameters NOT Used
docs.text:
• transfer.warehouse.externalDirectory / transfer.warehouse.managedDirectory -- Not used because locations are not translated.
  • transfer.targetNamespace -- Not used because the source namespace is preserved.
  • transfer.storageMigration.translationType -- Not applicable.
  • migrateACID.* -- ACID tables are rejected by LINKED.
  • migrateVIEW.on -- Views are not supported.
  • migrateNonNative -- Non-native tables are not supported.

docs.title: Where This Strategy Is NOT Applicable
docs.text:
• ACID/transactional tables -- ACID tables use internal delta file structures that cannot be read as external tables. The metastore transaction state is cluster-specific.
  • Views -- View SQL references tables by name. If those tables do not exist on the RIGHT cluster or exist under different names, the view will fail.
  • When clusters do not share storage access -- If the RIGHT cluster cannot reach LEFT's HDFS/S3/ABFS, the LINKED tables will fail at query time with file-not-found errors.
  • When data isolation is required -- Both clusters share the same physical data. If the RIGHT cluster could accidentally modify or delete data, LINKED is unsafe.
  • When you want RIGHT to own its own copy of the data -- Use SCHEMA_ONLY (with external distcp) or SQL/HYBRID (with engine-driven data movement) instead.
  • Long-term production use -- LINKED creates a dependency on the source cluster's storage. It is designed for testing and validation, not as a permanent architecture.

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
1. Hive 3 testing -- Set up a CDP test cluster that queries HDP production data without copying terabytes of data. Run your existing HiveQL workloads against the CDP engine and identify compatibility issues (deprecated UDFs, behavioral changes, performance regressions) before committing to a full migration.

  2. Gradual migration -- Start with LINKED for immediate access on the target cluster, then later use CONVERT_LINKED to take ownership after the data has been separately copied (via distcp, S3 replication, etc.). This two-phase approach provides immediate value while the data copy runs in the background.

  3. Cross-cluster analytics -- Allow a secondary analytics or reporting cluster to query primary cluster data without duplicating storage. Useful when a dedicated cluster is needed for heavy analytical workloads that should not impact production.

  4. Migration validation -- Create linked tables, run a suite of validation queries, and compare results with the source cluster. This confirms that the target Hive engine produces identical results before switching production workloads.

docs.title: (a) Simple External Table Linked to HDFS Source
docs.text:
Source (LEFT, legacy Hive on hdfs://HDP50): 

-- Original table on source cluster (legacy managed, non-ACID)
CREATE TABLE `sales.db`.`customers`(
  `c_id` bigint,
  `c_name` varchar(100),
  `c_email` varchar(200),
  `c_signup_date` date)
ROW FORMAT SERDE
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcSerde'
STORED AS INPUTFORMAT
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcInputFormat'
OUTPUTFORMAT
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcOutputFormat'
LOCATION
  'hdfs://HDP50/apps/hive/warehouse/sales.db/customers';

Generated RIGHT SQL (target CDP cluster, locations NOT translated): 

USE sales;

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE `customers`(
  `c_id` bigint,
  `c_name` varchar(100),
  `c_email` varchar(200),
  `c_signup_date` date)
ROW FORMAT SERDE
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcSerde'
STORED AS INPUTFORMAT
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcInputFormat'
OUTPUTFORMAT
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcOutputFormat'
LOCATION
  'hdfs://HDP50/apps/hive/warehouse/sales.db/customers'
TBLPROPERTIES (
  'hmsMirror_Metadata_Stage1'='2026-02-16 10:30:00',
  'hmsMirror_LegacyManaged'='true',
  'hmsMirror_Converted'='true',
  'discover.partitions'='true');

Note: The LOCATION still points to hdfs://HDP50 (the source cluster). The CDP cluster must have HDFS access to this nameservice. EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE is NOT present because the target does not own the data.

docs.title: (b) Partitioned Table with MSCK REPAIR
docs.text:
Source (LEFT): partitioned external table on the source cluster.

Generated RIGHT SQL: 

USE sales;

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE `orders`(
  `o_id` bigint,
  `o_customer_id` bigint,
  `o_total` decimal(12,2),
  `o_status` varchar(20))
PARTITIONED BY (
  `o_order_date` string)
ROW FORMAT SERDE
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcSerde'
STORED AS INPUTFORMAT
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcInputFormat'
OUTPUTFORMAT
  'org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.orc.OrcOutputFormat'
LOCATION
  'hdfs://HDP50/apps/hive/warehouse/sales.db/orders'
TBLPROPERTIES (
  'hmsMirror_Metadata_Stage1'='2026-02-16 10:30:00',
  'hmsMirror_LegacyManaged'='true',
  'hmsMirror_Converted'='true',
  'discover.partitions'='true');

MSCK REPAIR TABLE sales.orders;

Note: The MSCK REPAIR TABLE command discovers all partitions under the source LOCATION by scanning the directory structure for o_order_date=<value>/ subdirectories. This requires the target cluster to have read access to hdfs://HDP50.

docs.title: Relationship to CONVERT_LINKED
docs.text:
After using LINKED, the CONVERT_LINKED strategy converts these linked schemas to owned schemas by translating locations from the source namespace to the target namespace. This supports a two-phase migration workflow:

Phase 1: LINKED                         Phase 2: CONVERT_LINKED
+-------------------------------+       +----------------------------------+
| Create tables on RIGHT        |       | Translate locations to RIGHT     |
| pointing to LEFT's data       |       | namespace                        |
| (immediate read access)       |       | Set PURGE flag where appropriate |
+-------------------------------+       +----------------------------------+
          |                                        |
          v                                        v
  RIGHT queries LEFT data             RIGHT queries its own data copy
  (data copy runs in background)      (source cluster can be retired)

The CONVERT_LINKED process: - For non-partitioned tables: issues ALTER TABLE SET LOCATION to the translated target path, and adds EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE=true if the table was originally a legacy managed table. - For partitioned tables: drops and recreates the table using SCHEMA_ONLY (because each partition location must be individually translated, which cannot be done with ALTER TABLE alone).

This two-phase approach provides immediate value (queries work on day one via LINKED) while allowing the potentially long-running data copy to proceed independently.

docs.title: Safety Warnings
docs.text:
• LINKED is intended for testing and validation only. Tables on the RIGHT cluster point to LEFT cluster data. Dropping tables on RIGHT with EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE=true set would delete data on LEFT. LINKED explicitly prevents this by never setting the PURGE flag.
  • Always use readOnly=true and noPurge=true as defense-in-depth when running LINKED migrations.
  • DROP DATABASE CASCADE on RIGHT could still trigger data deletion if any table on RIGHT has PURGE set from a previous non-LINKED operation. Verify table properties before running destructive commands.
  • LINKED tables create a runtime dependency on the source cluster's storage. If the source cluster goes down or the storage becomes inaccessible, all LINKED tables on the target will fail at query time.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Migrates table data from source to target using SQL INSERT...SELECT through HiveServer2. Creates an intermediate SHADOW table on the target cluster that points to the source's data, then uses a local INSERT INTO...SELECT to transfer data to the final target table. This keeps all SQL execution on the target cluster.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Create SHADOW table on RIGHT -- an external table pointing to LEFT's data location (no translation)
  2. Create final target table on RIGHT -- with translated location in target namespace
  3. Execute INSERT INTO target SELECT * FROM shadow -- data flows through HiveServer2
  4. Drop SHADOW table (cleanup, unless saveWorkingTables=true)

The SHADOW table is key: it is a lightweight external table on the RIGHT cluster that gives RIGHT's HiveServer2 access to LEFT's data. This means only ONE cluster (RIGHT) executes SQL -- there is no cross-cluster SQL coordination.

LEFT Cluster                           RIGHT Cluster
+------------------+                   +----------------------------------+
| Source Table     |                   | SHADOW Table                     |
| (original data)  |                   | (external, points to LEFT data)  |
|                  |                   |                                  |
| hdfs://left/     | <-- reads from -- | LOCATION 'hdfs://left/...'       |
| warehouse/db/tbl |                   +----------------------------------+
+------------------+                              |
                                        INSERT INTO...SELECT *
                                                  |
                                                  v
                                       +----------------------------------+
                                       | Target Table                     |
                                       | (translated location)            |
                                       |                                  |
                                       | LOCATION 's3a://target/...'      |
                                       +----------------------------------+

docs.title: Routing to Other Strategies
docs.text:
The SQL strategy delegates to other implementations based on configuration:

  • If intermediateStorage or targetNamespace is configured, delegates to IntermediateDataStrategy
  • If the source table is ACID and migrateACID.inplace is set, delegates to SQLAcidInPlaceDataStrategy
  • If the source table is ACID and migrateACID.on is set, delegates to IntermediateDataStrategy

docs.title: Schema Translation Details
docs.text:
• SHADOW table: uses LEFT's original location (no translation), marked with HMS_MIRROR_SHADOW_TABLE property
  • Target table: full location translation to target namespace
  • Managed-to-External conversion: applies when migrating from legacy Hive (2.x) to non-legacy Hive (3.x). Managed non-transactional tables become EXTERNAL TABLE with EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE=true to preserve data ownership semantics
  • ACID handling: when migrateACID.downgrade is enabled, ACID tables are converted to external. When ACID tables are detected, the strategy routes to IntermediateDataStrategy for proper handling
  • Partition handling for INSERT:
  • Dynamic partitioning is enabled via SET hive.exec.dynamic.partition=true and SET hive.exec.dynamic.partition.mode=nonstrict
  • Three optimization modes controlled by optimization.skip and optimization.sortDynamicPartitionInserts:
    • SKIP (optimization.skip=true): Forces all partitions via declarative INSERT OVERWRITE TABLE ... PARTITION (cols) SELECT *. Sets hive.optimize.sort.dynamic.partition=false
    • SORT_DYNAMIC_PARTITION (optimization.sortDynamicPartitionInserts=true): Enables partition sorting via SET hive.optimize.sort.dynamic.partition=true and SET hive.optimize.sort.dynamic.partition.threshold=0. Uses declarative partition clause
    • PRESCRIPTIVE (default): Uses explicit DISTRIBUTE BY for partition columns via INSERT OVERWRITE TABLE ... PARTITION (cols) SELECT * DISTRIBUTE BY partCols. Sets hive.optimize.sort.dynamic.partition=false and hive.optimize.sort.dynamic.partition.threshold=-1

docs.title: Properties Managed
docs.text: Property Action Purpose HMS_MIRROR_METADATA_FLAG Added to RIGHT Timestamp marking migration HMS_MIRROR_SHADOW_TABLE Added to SHADOW Identifies shadow tables for cleanup EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE Added to RIGHT Data ownership on converted managed tables DISCOVER_PARTITIONS Added to RIGHT Enables auto partition discovery DOWNGRADED_FROM_ACID Added to RIGHT Marks ACID-to-external conversions COLUMN_STATS_ACCURATE Removed Stale stats from source numFiles, numRows, etc. Removed Stale metrics from source transient_lastDdlTime Removed Source timestamp, irrelevant on target

docs.title: Data Mapping
docs.text:
• Data flows: LEFT storage -> (read by RIGHT's HiveServer2 via SHADOW) -> RIGHT storage
  • All data passes through RIGHT cluster's compute resources (Tez/MapReduce)
  • Data format may change (e.g., source ORC -> target Parquet if Iceberg conversion is enabled)
  • Non-partitioned tables use FROM shadow INSERT OVERWRITE TABLE target SELECT *
  • Partitioned tables include partition columns in SELECT and map them via INSERT INTO...PARTITION clause
  • MSCK REPAIR TABLE is run on the SHADOW table to register source partitions on RIGHT before the INSERT

docs.title: Configuration Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Default Effect hybrid.sqlPartitionLimit 500 Maximum number of partitions before the strategy fails with an error. Prevents OOM/timeout on very large partitioned tables optimization.sortDynamicPartitionInserts false Enables hive.optimize.sort.dynamic.partition=true for partition sort optimization optimization.skip false Forces declarative partition mode, disabling sort optimization optimization.overrides (none) Custom SET statements injected before INSERT. Keyed by environment (LEFT/RIGHT) transfer.shadowPrefix hms_mirror_shadow_ Prefix for shadow table name. Table orders becomes hms_mirror_shadow_orders saveWorkingTables false When true, keeps shadow table after migration for debugging/validation icebergConversion.enable false Converts target to Iceberg format during INSERT. Sets iceberg.mr.schema.auto.conversion=true icebergConversion.version 2 Iceberg format version (1 or 2) migrateACID.on false Enables ACID table migration (routes to Intermediate strategy) migrateACID.downgrade false Enables ACID-to-external conversion migrateACID.inplace false Enables in-place ACID downgrade on source cluster migrateACID.artificialBucketThreshold 2 Removes CLUSTERED BY if bucket count is at or below this threshold

docs.title: Where This Strategy Is NOT Applicable
docs.text:
• Clusters without shared storage access -- RIGHT must be able to read LEFT's data to create the SHADOW table. If RIGHT cannot reach hdfs://left-cluster/..., the SHADOW table is useless
  • Very large tables -- HiveServer2 memory and time constraints make SQL INSERT impractical for multi-TB tables. The entire dataset flows through HiveServer2's JVM
  • ACID tables between incompatible Hive versions -- Legacy ACID (Hive 1/2) uses a different storage format than Hive 3 ACID. Use EXPORT_IMPORT or HYBRID instead
  • Tables exceeding sqlPartitionLimit -- The strategy will abort with an error. HYBRID auto-routes these to EXPORT_IMPORT
  • Network-isolated clusters -- If there is no network path from RIGHT compute nodes to LEFT storage, the INSERT via SHADOW will fail
  • When intermediateStorage is configured -- The strategy automatically delegates to IntermediateDataStrategy, which uses a different transfer mechanism

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
1. Small-to-medium table migration -- Tables under a few GB where SQL INSERT through HiveServer2 completes in a reasonable time. The overhead of EXPORT/IMPORT's file copy is unnecessary for small datasets
  2. Format conversion during migration -- Change from ORC to Parquet or convert to Iceberg in a single step. The INSERT...SELECT re-serializes data in the target table's format
  3. Partition consolidation -- Dynamic partitioning can reorganize partition layouts during INSERT. The PRESCRIPTIVE mode with DISTRIBUTE BY gives explicit control over partition distribution
  4. ACID downgrade with data -- Combine ACID-to-external conversion with data movement in one operation. The source ACID table's data is read through the SHADOW table and written as non-transactional data to the target
  5. Development/test environment seeding -- Copy production data via SQL. Use optimization.overrides to inject custom SET statements (e.g., resource limits, queue assignments)

docs.title: (a) Non-Partitioned Table
docs.text:
A simple external table sales.orders being migrated from an HDP cluster to CDP with S3 storage.

-- Executed on RIGHT cluster
USE sales;

-- Drop any previous shadow table
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS hms_mirror_shadow_orders;

-- Create SHADOW table pointing to LEFT's data
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE hms_mirror_shadow_orders (
  order_id    BIGINT,
  customer_id BIGINT,
  order_date  STRING,
  total       DECIMAL(10,2)
)
STORED AS ORC
LOCATION 'hdfs://HDP-source/warehouse/sales.db/orders'
TBLPROPERTIES (
  'HMS_MIRROR_SHADOW_TABLE'='true'
);

-- Create final target table with translated location
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE orders (
  order_id    BIGINT,
  customer_id BIGINT,
  order_date  STRING,
  total       DECIMAL(10,2)
)
STORED AS ORC
LOCATION 's3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/sales.db/orders'
TBLPROPERTIES (
  'HMS_MIRROR_METADATA_FLAG'='2024-03-15 10:30:00',
  'external.table.purge'='true',
  'hmsMirror_LegacyManaged'='true',
  'hmsMirror_Converted'='true'
);

-- Transfer data via SQL INSERT
FROM hms_mirror_shadow_orders INSERT OVERWRITE TABLE orders SELECT *;

-- Cleanup (runs after successful migration)
USE sales;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS hms_mirror_shadow_orders;

docs.title: (b) Partitioned Table with Dynamic Partitions and Sort Optimization
docs.text:
A partitioned table analytics.web_events with 150 partitions, using sortDynamicPartitionInserts.

-- Executed on RIGHT cluster
USE analytics;

-- Drop any previous shadow table
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS hms_mirror_shadow_web_events;

-- Create SHADOW table pointing to LEFT's data
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE hms_mirror_shadow_web_events (
  event_id    BIGINT,
  user_id     BIGINT,
  event_type  STRING,
  payload     STRING
)
PARTITIONED BY (event_date STRING, region STRING)
STORED AS ORC
LOCATION 'hdfs://HDP-source/warehouse/analytics.db/web_events'
TBLPROPERTIES (
  'HMS_MIRROR_SHADOW_TABLE'='true'
);

-- Repair partitions on the shadow table so RIGHT can see LEFT's partitions
MSCK REPAIR TABLE hms_mirror_shadow_web_events;

-- Create final target table
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE web_events (
  event_id    BIGINT,
  user_id     BIGINT,
  event_type  STRING,
  payload     STRING
)
PARTITIONED BY (event_date STRING, region STRING)
STORED AS ORC
LOCATION 's3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/analytics.db/web_events'
TBLPROPERTIES (
  'HMS_MIRROR_METADATA_FLAG'='2024-03-15 10:30:00',
  'external.table.purge'='true',
  'discover.partitions'='true'
);

-- Enable sort optimization for dynamic partitions
SET hive.optimize.sort.dynamic.partition=true;
SET hive.optimize.sort.dynamic.partition.threshold=0;

-- Transfer data with declarative dynamic partitions
FROM hms_mirror_shadow_web_events
INSERT OVERWRITE TABLE web_events PARTITION (event_date, region)
SELECT *;

-- Cleanup
USE analytics;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS hms_mirror_shadow_web_events;

docs.title: (c) ACID Downgrade Scenario
docs.text:
An ACID managed table finance.transactions being downgraded to an external table. Since this is an ACID table, the SQL strategy routes to the IntermediateDataStrategy, which uses a TRANSFER table on the LEFT and a SHADOW table on the RIGHT.

-- Phase 1: Executed on LEFT cluster
USE finance;

-- Create transfer (external) table from ACID source on LEFT
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE hms_mirror_transfer_transactions (
  txn_id      BIGINT,
  account_id  BIGINT,
  amount      DECIMAL(12,2),
  txn_type    STRING,
  txn_date    STRING
)
STORED AS ORC
LOCATION 'hdfs://HDP-source/apps/hive/warehouse/export_finance/transactions'
TBLPROPERTIES (
  'HMS_MIRROR_TRANSFER_TABLE'='true',
  'external.table.purge'='true'
);

-- Move ACID data into the external transfer table
SET hive.optimize.sort.dynamic.partition=false;
SET hive.optimize.sort.dynamic.partition.threshold=-1;
FROM transactions INSERT OVERWRITE TABLE hms_mirror_transfer_transactions SELECT *;

-- Phase 2: Executed on RIGHT cluster
USE finance;

-- Create shadow table on RIGHT pointing to the transfer location on LEFT
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS hms_mirror_shadow_transactions;
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE hms_mirror_shadow_transactions (
  txn_id      BIGINT,
  account_id  BIGINT,
  amount      DECIMAL(12,2),
  txn_type    STRING,
  txn_date    STRING
)
STORED AS ORC
LOCATION 'hdfs://HDP-source/apps/hive/warehouse/export_finance/transactions'
TBLPROPERTIES (
  'HMS_MIRROR_SHADOW_TABLE'='true'
);

-- Create final target as EXTERNAL (downgraded from ACID)
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE transactions (
  txn_id      BIGINT,
  account_id  BIGINT,
  amount      DECIMAL(12,2),
  txn_type    STRING,
  txn_date    STRING
)
STORED AS ORC
LOCATION 's3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/finance.db/transactions'
TBLPROPERTIES (
  'HMS_MIRROR_METADATA_FLAG'='2024-03-15 10:30:00',
  'external.table.purge'='true',
  'DOWNGRADED_FROM_ACID'='true'
);

-- Transfer data from shadow to target
FROM hms_mirror_shadow_transactions INSERT OVERWRITE TABLE transactions SELECT *;

-- Cleanup on LEFT
USE finance;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS hms_mirror_transfer_transactions;

-- Cleanup on RIGHT
USE finance;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS hms_mirror_shadow_transactions;

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Uses Hive's native EXPORT TABLE / IMPORT TABLE commands to move data between clusters. The EXPORT command writes table data files and a _metadata file to an intermediate storage location, and IMPORT reads it on the target cluster to reconstruct the table. This is the only strategy that natively supports ACID table data movement between compatible Hive versions.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Execute EXPORT TABLE on LEFT -- writes data files and _metadata file to export directory
  2. Execute IMPORT EXTERNAL TABLE on RIGHT -- reads from the export directory, creates table with translated location
  3. For ACID downgrade: exports to an archive-suffixed intermediate table first
LEFT Cluster                        Shared Storage                     RIGHT Cluster
+------------------+               +---------------------+            +------------------+
| Source Table     |   EXPORT TO   | Export Directory     |  IMPORT   | Target Table     |
| (original data)  | -----------> | /{prefix}/{db}/{tbl} | --------> | (translated loc) |
|                  |               |   _metadata          |           |                  |
| hdfs://left/     |               |   data files...      |           | s3a://target/    |
| warehouse/db/tbl |               +---------------------+            | warehouse/db/tbl |
+------------------+                                                  +------------------+

The export directory path is constructed as: {namespace}{exportBaseDirPrefix}{database}/{table}

Where {namespace} is determined by: - If intermediateStorage is configured: the intermediate storage URI - If targetNamespace is configured: the target namespace URI - Otherwise: the LEFT cluster's namespace (from the source table's location)

docs.title: ACID Table Handling
docs.text:
For ACID tables, Hive's EXPORT natively handles the transactional delta files. The critical constraint is version compatibility: both LEFT and RIGHT must be on the same major Hive version (both legacy or both non-legacy). The strategy explicitly checks this and fails with an error if there is a mismatch:

Can't process ACID tables with EXPORT_IMPORT between 'legacy' and 'non-legacy'
hive environments. The processes aren't compatible.

For ACID in-place downgrade, the strategy delegates to ExportImportAcidDowngradeInPlaceDataStrategy, which: 1. Renames the original ACID table to {tableName}_archive 2. Exports the archived table 3. Imports it back as an external table at the original name

docs.title: Schema Translation Details
docs.text:
• Export location: determined by exportBaseDirPrefix, intermediateStorage, or targetNamespace configuration
  • Import location: translated to target namespace via the Translator. For non-ACID tables, the IMPORT EXTERNAL TABLE ... LOCATION form is used to explicitly set the target path
  • ACID tables: natively supported when both clusters run compatible Hive versions (both legacy Hive 1/2 or both non-legacy Hive 3+). Imported using IMPORT TABLE (not IMPORT EXTERNAL TABLE) to preserve transactional properties
  • ACID downgrade: exports the original ACID table, imports as EXTERNAL TABLE. The DOWNGRADED_FROM_ACID property is added and EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE is set for data ownership
  • External tables: always imported with IMPORT EXTERNAL TABLE ... LOCATION to ensure proper location mapping
  • Partition metadata: preserved through the export/import cycle. The _metadata file captures full partition specifications
  • Table properties: preserved through export, minus stripped statistics (COLUMN_STATS_ACCURATE, numFiles, numRows, rawDataSize, totalSize, transient_lastDdlTime)
  • Managed-to-External conversion: when migrating from legacy to non-legacy Hive, managed non-transactional tables are converted to EXTERNAL TABLE with EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE=true
  • Ownership transfer: ALTER TABLE ... SET OWNER USER is executed on the RIGHT if ownership transfer is enabled and the RIGHT cluster is non-legacy Hive

docs.title: Location Alignment Checking
docs.text:
When using ALIGNED translation type, the strategy verifies that the target table location falls within the database's configured location. If the table location does not align with the database location, the migration fails with an error:

Table location {location} is not aligned with the database location
using translation type: ALIGNED

For RELATIVE translation type, a warning is issued but the migration proceeds.

docs.title: Data Mapping
docs.text:
• Data flows: LEFT storage -> export directory -> RIGHT storage
  • Export directory must be accessible by BOTH clusters (shared HDFS namespace, S3 bucket, ADLS container, etc.)
  • File format is preserved exactly (ORC stays ORC, Parquet stays Parquet). No serialization/deserialization occurs
  • ACID delta files are handled natively by Hive's export mechanism -- compacted and written as standard data files in the export directory
  • intermediateStorage enables multi-hop: Source cluster -> cloud bucket -> Target cluster. Both clusters must be able to access the intermediate location independently
  • The remoteWorkingDirectory (default: hms_mirror_working) is appended to intermediate storage paths to isolate migration artifacts

docs.title: Configuration Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Default Effect hybrid.exportImportPartitionLimit 100 Maximum number of partitions before the strategy fails. Prevents runaway exports on heavily partitioned tables transfer.exportBaseDirPrefix /apps/hive/warehouse/export_ Base directory path for export output. Each table's export is written to {namespace}{prefix}{database}/{table} transfer.remoteWorkingDirectory hms_mirror_working Subdirectory name used under intermediate/target storage for temporary artifacts transfer.intermediateStorage (none) Fully-qualified URI for multi-hop migrations (e.g., s3a://migration-bucket/staging/). When set, the export directory is written here instead of on the LEFT cluster's filesystem migrateACID.on false Enables ACID table migration via EXPORT/IMPORT migrateACID.downgrade false Converts ACID tables to EXTERNAL+PURGE during import migrateACID.inplace false Performs ACID downgrade on the source cluster itself (renames original, exports, imports back as external) warehouse.external (none) Target warehouse directory for external tables. Used for location translation during IMPORT warehouse.managed (none) Target warehouse directory for managed tables ownershipTransfer.table false When true, runs ALTER TABLE SET OWNER after import to preserve ownership forceExternalLocation false Always specifies explicit LOCATION in the IMPORT statement createIfNotExists true Adds IF NOT EXISTS semantics to the schema check

docs.title: Where This Strategy Is NOT Applicable
docs.text:
• ACID tables between legacy Hive (1/2) and non-legacy Hive (3+) -- The ACID storage formats are incompatible. Hive 2 ACID uses a different delta file structure than Hive 3 ACID. The EXPORT from one version cannot be IMPORT-ed by the other. Use SQL strategy with ACID downgrade instead
  • Clusters with no shared storage for the export directory -- Both clusters must be able to read the export directory. If cluster A writes to hdfs://clusterA/export/ and cluster B cannot reach that path, the IMPORT will fail. Use intermediateStorage (e.g., S3) to bridge the gap
  • Very large tables where export time exceeds acceptable window -- EXPORT copies all data files to the export directory. For multi-TB tables, this can be slow. Consider SCHEMA_ONLY with distcp for very large datasets
  • When format conversion is needed -- EXPORT preserves file format exactly. If you need to convert ORC to Parquet, use the SQL strategy instead
  • When Iceberg conversion is desired -- EXPORT/IMPORT does not support Iceberg table format. Use the SQL strategy with icebergConversion.enable=true
  • Views and non-native tables -- The strategy explicitly rejects views, HBase-backed tables, Kafka tables, and other non-native Hive tables with the error: Can't process ACID tables, VIEWs, or Non Native Hive Tables with this strategy

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
1. ACID table migration -- The only native approach for moving transactional table data between compatible Hive clusters. Hive's EXPORT mechanism handles delta files, compaction state, and transaction metadata natively. No other strategy preserves ACID semantics without downgrading
  2. Exact data fidelity -- When you need bit-for-bit identical data files on the target. EXPORT copies data files directly without serialization/deserialization through SQL, eliminating any risk of floating-point drift, encoding changes, or implicit type coercion
  3. Network-isolated clusters with shared cloud storage -- Export to S3 from on-prem cluster A, import from S3 on cluster B. Configure intermediateStorage to point to the shared cloud bucket. The two clusters never need direct network connectivity
  4. Large table migration -- More efficient than SQL for large tables because EXPORT performs a direct file copy rather than row-by-row INSERT...SELECT through HiveServer2. The data does not pass through the JVM heap
  5. Cross-version migration with intermediate storage -- HDP cluster exports to S3 using intermediateStorage, CDP cluster imports from S3. This is the standard pattern for HDP-to-CDP migrations where the clusters have no shared HDFS namespace

docs.title: (a) Basic Non-ACID Table Export/Import
docs.text:
An external table sales.orders being migrated from HDP to CDP. Both clusters can access the LEFT cluster's HDFS namespace.

-- Executed on LEFT cluster
USE sales;
EXPORT TABLE orders TO "hdfs://HDP-source/apps/hive/warehouse/export_sales/orders";

-- Executed on RIGHT cluster
USE sales;
IMPORT EXTERNAL TABLE orders
  FROM "hdfs://HDP-source/apps/hive/warehouse/export_sales/orders"
  LOCATION "s3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/sales.db/orders";
ALTER TABLE orders SET OWNER USER data_engineering;

docs.title: (b) ACID Table Export/Import
docs.text:
A transactional table finance.transactions being migrated between two CDP clusters running compatible Hive 3 versions. The ACID format is preserved.

-- Executed on LEFT cluster (CDP)
USE finance;
EXPORT TABLE transactions
  TO "hdfs://CDP-source/apps/hive/warehouse/export_finance/transactions";

-- Executed on RIGHT cluster (CDP)
USE finance;
IMPORT TABLE transactions
  FROM "hdfs://CDP-source/apps/hive/warehouse/export_finance/transactions";

Note: IMPORT TABLE (not IMPORT EXTERNAL TABLE) is used to preserve transactional properties. The ACID delta files in the export directory are read natively by the target cluster's Hive.

docs.title: (c) Multi-Hop with intermediateStorage
docs.text:
An external table analytics.web_events being migrated from an on-prem HDP cluster to CDP in the cloud. The clusters cannot see each other's HDFS, but both can access an S3 bucket.

-- Configuration:
--   intermediateStorage: s3a://migration-bucket/staging/

-- Executed on LEFT cluster (HDP, on-prem)
USE analytics;
EXPORT TABLE web_events
  TO "s3a://migration-bucket/staging/hms_mirror_working/job_20240315/analytics/web_events";

-- Executed on RIGHT cluster (CDP, cloud)
USE analytics;
IMPORT EXTERNAL TABLE web_events
  FROM "s3a://migration-bucket/staging/hms_mirror_working/job_20240315/analytics/web_events"
  LOCATION "s3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/analytics.db/web_events";

The intermediate S3 path includes the remoteWorkingDirectory (hms_mirror_working), the job key, database name, and table name to prevent collisions between concurrent migration jobs.

docs.title: (d) ACID Downgrade via Export/Import
docs.text:
A transactional (ACID) table finance.ledger on HDP (legacy Hive) being migrated to CDP (non-legacy Hive). Since the ACID formats are incompatible across Hive versions, the table is downgraded to EXTERNAL with in-place downgrade on the LEFT.

-- Executed on LEFT cluster (HDP, legacy Hive)
USE finance;

-- Remove property that would prevent rename
ALTER TABLE ledger UNSET TBLPROPERTIES ("TRANSLATED_TO_EXTERNAL");

-- Rename original ACID table to archive name
ALTER TABLE ledger RENAME TO ledger_archive;

-- Export the archived ACID table
EXPORT TABLE ledger_archive
  TO "hdfs://HDP-source/apps/hive/warehouse/export_finance/ledger_archive";

-- Import back as EXTERNAL table (downgraded)
IMPORT EXTERNAL TABLE ledger
  FROM "hdfs://HDP-source/apps/hive/warehouse/export_finance/ledger_archive";

-- On RIGHT cluster (CDP, non-legacy Hive), if not in-place:
USE finance;

-- Drop existing table if schema sync is active
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS ledger;

IMPORT EXTERNAL TABLE ledger
  FROM "hdfs://HDP-source/apps/hive/warehouse/export_finance/ledger"
  LOCATION "s3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/finance.db/ledger";
ALTER TABLE ledger SET OWNER USER finance_team;

When migrateACID.inplace=true, both the EXPORT and IMPORT happen on the LEFT cluster -- the original ACID table is renamed, exported, and re-imported as an external table. The RIGHT cluster is not involved.

When migrateACID.inplace=false (cross-cluster downgrade), the EXPORT happens on LEFT and the IMPORT happens on RIGHT with IMPORT EXTERNAL TABLE to create a non-transactional copy.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Data Strategy Concept
  • SQL Strategy
  • HYBRID Strategy

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Automatically selects between SQL and EXPORT_IMPORT on a per-table basis, optimizing for each table's characteristics. Large partitioned tables route to SQL (which handles dynamic partitioning efficiently), while small/unpartitioned tables and ACID tables route to EXPORT_IMPORT.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
For each table, HYBRID evaluates:

IF table is ACID AND legacy-to-non-legacy migration:
    -> Use INTERMEDIATE (forced, format incompatibility)
ELSE IF table is partitioned AND partition count > exportImportPartitionLimit:
    -> Use SQL (handles large partition counts better)
    -> OR Use INTERMEDIATE if intermediateStorage or target namespace override is set
ELSE:
    -> Use EXPORT_IMPORT (default, handles most cases well)

The strategy delegates to the actual SQL, EXPORT_IMPORT, or INTERMEDIATE strategy implementation. HYBRID is a router, not its own execution engine. It does not override buildOutDefinition() or buildOutSql() directly; instead, its build() and execute() methods dispatch to the selected delegate strategy.

docs.title: Selection Criteria Details
docs.text:
• Partition count is the primary routing metric for non-ACID tables.
  • exportImportPartitionLimit (default: 100) is the threshold. Tables with partition counts at or below this limit use EXPORT_IMPORT. Tables above this limit use SQL.
  • sqlPartitionLimit (default: 500) is the upper safety valve. Tables exceeding this limit are flagged for manual handling or skipped entirely.
  • ACID tables in a legacy-to-non-legacy migration always route to INTERMEDIATE (format incompatibility between Hive 1/2 ACID and Hive 3 ACID requires an intermediate step). If migrateACID is not enabled, ACID tables produce an error.
  • Non-partitioned tables always route to EXPORT_IMPORT (simpler, more reliable for small/medium datasets).
  • When intermediateStorage is configured or a target namespace override is present, tables that would normally route to SQL instead route to INTERMEDIATE (SQL_INTERMEDIATE), because direct cross-namespace SQL is not possible without a bridge.

docs.title: Schema Translation Details
docs.text: Same as the delegated strategy (SQL, EXPORT_IMPORT, or INTERMEDIATE). The HYBRID strategy itself does not modify any translation behavior. Location translation, table property management, and type conversions are all handled by the delegate.

docs.title: Data Mapping
docs.text:
Same as the delegated strategy. Data flows depend on whether SQL, EXPORT_IMPORT, or INTERMEDIATE was selected:

  • SQL route: LEFT storage -> SHADOW table on RIGHT (points to LEFT's data) -> INSERT INTO RIGHT table -> RIGHT storage
  • EXPORT_IMPORT route: LEFT storage -> EXPORT to directory -> IMPORT on RIGHT -> RIGHT storage
  • INTERMEDIATE route (ACID or cross-namespace): LEFT storage -> TRANSFER staging table -> INSERT INTO RIGHT table -> RIGHT storage

docs.title: Configuration Parameters
docs.text:
Parameter Description Default hybrid.exportImportPartitionLimit Partition count threshold. Tables at or below this use EXPORT_IMPORT; above this use SQL. 100 hybrid.sqlPartitionLimit Maximum partitions for the SQL route. Tables exceeding this are skipped (safety valve). 500 hybrid.sqlSizeLimit Maximum estimated table size (bytes) for the SQL route. 1073741824 (1 GB) intermediateStorage If set, ACID tables and large-partition tables route through INTERMEDIATE strategy. (none) migrateACID.on Enables ACID table migration. Without this, ACID tables in HYBRID produce an error. false migrateACID.downgrade Convert ACID to EXTERNAL+PURGE during migration. false

All SQL strategy parameters apply when the SQL route is selected. All EXPORT_IMPORT strategy parameters apply when the EXPORT_IMPORT route is selected. All INTERMEDIATE strategy parameters apply when the INTERMEDIATE route is selected.

docs.title: Where This Strategy Is NOT Applicable
docs.text:
• When you need predictable, uniform behavior across all tables (use SQL or EXPORT_IMPORT directly).
  • Single-cluster storage migration (use STORAGE_MIGRATION).
  • Schema-only operations (use SCHEMA_ONLY).
  • When clusters do not share storage access (neither SQL nor EXPORT_IMPORT will work without a shared filesystem or intermediate storage).
  • When you need to migrate views (HYBRID does not handle views).

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
1. Production database migration -- Mixed workloads with tables from 1 KB to 1 TB, some ACID, some partitioned. HYBRID routes each table to the optimal strategy automatically.

  2. HDP to CDP migration -- Handles the full spectrum: non-ACID tables with few partitions via EXPORT_IMPORT, non-ACID tables with many partitions via SQL, and legacy ACID tables via INTERMEDIATE.

  3. Hands-off migration -- When you do not want to manually categorize tables by strategy. HYBRID makes reasonable choices based on partition counts and table types.

  4. Mixed partition density -- Databases with both heavily-partitioned (1,000+ partitions) and simple tables benefit from per-table routing rather than a one-size-fits-all strategy.

docs.title: (a) Table Routed to SQL -- Large Partitioned Table
docs.text:
A partitioned table with 250 partitions exceeds the exportImportPartitionLimit of 100, so HYBRID routes it to the SQL strategy.

-- Routed to SQL: partition count (250) > exportImportPartitionLimit (100)

-- On RIGHT cluster: Create shadow table pointing to LEFT's storage
USE target_db;
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS hms_mirror_shadow_orders
LIKE source_db.orders
LOCATION 'hdfs://source-cluster/warehouse/source_db.db/orders';

-- On RIGHT cluster: Create the final target table
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS orders (
    order_id    BIGINT,
    customer_id BIGINT,
    order_date  STRING,
    amount      DECIMAL(10,2)
)
PARTITIONED BY (region STRING)
STORED AS ORC
LOCATION 's3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/target_db.db/orders'
TBLPROPERTIES (
    'HMS_MIRROR_METADATA_FLAG'='2026-02-16 10:00:00',
    'EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE'='true'
);

-- On RIGHT cluster: Move data via SQL
FROM hms_mirror_shadow_orders
INSERT OVERWRITE TABLE orders PARTITION (region)
SELECT *;

-- Cleanup: Drop shadow table
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS hms_mirror_shadow_orders;

docs.title: (b) Table Routed to EXPORT_IMPORT -- Small/Unpartitioned Table
docs.text:
An unpartitioned table (or a table with 50 partitions, which is below the 100 threshold) routes to EXPORT_IMPORT.

-- Routed to EXPORT_IMPORT: unpartitioned table (always uses EXPORT_IMPORT)

-- On LEFT cluster: Export table data
USE source_db;
EXPORT TABLE customers TO '/apps/hive/warehouse/export_hms_mirror/customers';

-- On RIGHT cluster: Import table data
USE target_db;
IMPORT EXTERNAL TABLE customers
FROM '/apps/hive/warehouse/export_hms_mirror/customers'
LOCATION 's3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/target_db.db/customers';

ALTER TABLE customers SET TBLPROPERTIES (
    'HMS_MIRROR_METADATA_FLAG'='2026-02-16 10:00:00',
    'EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE'='true'
);

docs.title: (c) ACID Table Routed to INTERMEDIATE (Legacy Migration with Downgrade)
docs.text:
An ACID table in a legacy-to-non-legacy (HDP 2 to CDP) migration is forced to the INTERMEDIATE strategy regardless of partition count. With migrateACID.downgrade enabled, the table is converted from ACID to EXTERNAL+PURGE.

-- Routed to INTERMEDIATE: ACID table in legacy-to-non-legacy migration
-- Downgrade: ACID -> EXTERNAL + PURGE

-- On LEFT cluster: Create transfer table (external, pointing to intermediate storage)
USE source_db;
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS hms_mirror_transfer_transactions (
    txn_id      BIGINT,
    account_id  BIGINT,
    amount      DECIMAL(10,2),
    txn_date    STRING
)
STORED AS ORC
LOCATION 's3a://intermediate-bucket/hms_mirror/source_db/transactions'
TBLPROPERTIES ('HMS_MIRROR_TRANSFER_TABLE'='true');

-- On LEFT cluster: Move data from ACID source to transfer table
FROM transactions
INSERT OVERWRITE TABLE hms_mirror_transfer_transactions
SELECT *;

-- On RIGHT cluster: Create target table (downgraded to external)
USE target_db;
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transactions (
    txn_id      BIGINT,
    account_id  BIGINT,
    amount      DECIMAL(10,2),
    txn_date    STRING
)
STORED AS ORC
LOCATION 's3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/target_db.db/transactions'
TBLPROPERTIES (
    'HMS_MIRROR_METADATA_FLAG'='2026-02-16 10:00:00',
    'EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE'='true',
    'DOWNGRADED_FROM_ACID'='true'
);

-- On RIGHT cluster: Load data from intermediate storage
FROM hms_mirror_transfer_transactions
INSERT OVERWRITE TABLE transactions
SELECT *;

-- Cleanup
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS hms_mirror_transfer_transactions;

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Migrates table data within a single cluster (or across clusters) from one storage namespace to another. This handles scenarios like HDFS-to-S3 migration, namespace changes, warehouse directory reorganization, and Ozone migration. Unlike other strategies that copy data between clusters, STORAGE_MIGRATION transforms tables in-place by renaming, recreating, and moving data on the same cluster.

docs.title: SQL Mode (default)
docs.text:
1. Add HMS_STORAGE_MIGRATION_FLAG property with timestamp to mark the table as being migrated.
  2. Remove the TRANSLATED_TO_EXTERNAL property if present (prevents rename conflicts).
  3. Rename original table to archive name (e.g., orders -> orders_storage_migration).
  4. Create new table with the original name pointing to the new (translated) storage location.
  5. INSERT OVERWRITE new table SELECT * from archive table (data movement via Hive SQL).
  6. Drop archive table after verification (generated as cleanup SQL).

For partitioned tables, the INSERT uses dynamic partitioning: 

FROM orders_storage_migration INSERT OVERWRITE TABLE orders PARTITION (region) SELECT *

docs.title: Distcp Mode (without archive)
docs.text:
When storageMigration.distcp is enabled and createArchive is false:

  1. Generate ALTER TABLE SET LOCATION for the table pointing to the new storage path.
  2. For partitioned tables, generate ALTER TABLE PARTITION (...) SET LOCATION for each partition individually.
  3. Generate distcp commands for external execution (data movement happens outside of Hive).
  4. No intermediate/archive table is needed; the table metadata is updated in-place.

docs.title: Distcp Mode (with archive)
docs.text:
When both storageMigration.distcp and createArchive are enabled:

  1. Rename the original table to the archive name.
  2. Create a new table at the target location.
  3. For partitioned tables, generate ALTER TABLE ADD PARTITION ... LOCATION for each partition with the new location.
  4. Generate distcp commands for data movement.
  5. The archive table preserves access to the original data at the source location.

All SQL is generated against the LEFT environment only, since STORAGE_MIGRATION operates on a single cluster.

docs.title: Schema Translation Details
docs.text:
• Location translation is driven by TranslationTypeEnum:
  • RELATIVE: Only the [protocol]://namespace prefix is changed. The relative path structure from the source is preserved. For example, hdfs://old-cluster/warehouse/db/table becomes s3a://bucket/warehouse/db/table.
  • ALIGNED: Full translation using the system-built location maps. Paths are restructured to align with warehouse directory conventions (e.g., <warehouse>/external/<db.db>/<table>).
  • Warehouse directories (external/managed) determine the target root path based on table type.
  • Partition locations are individually translated (each partition may have a different source path).
  • ACID tables: Location is stripped from the CREATE statement (Hive 3 manages ACID locations internally). If migrateACID.downgrade is set, the table is converted to EXTERNAL+PURGE.
  • HMS_STORAGE_MIGRATION_FLAG property is added with a timestamp to mark migrated tables.
  • Table type is preserved (external stays external, managed stays managed) unless explicitly downgraded.
  • If Iceberg conversion is enabled (icebergConversion.enable), Hive session variables are set to trigger the conversion during table creation.

docs.title: Data Mapping
docs.text:
• Data moves within the cluster (or to a new namespace accessible by the same cluster).
  • SQL mode: Data is serialized/deserialized through HiveServer2. This may change the file layout (e.g., rewriting ORC files, adjusting compression). Supports format transformation.
  • Distcp mode: Bit-for-bit file copy that preserves the exact file structure. No format transformation. Requires that partition locations match their partition spec directory structure for correct path translation.
  • ALIGNED mode validates that translated table paths match the warehouse convention (<warehouse>/<db.db>/<table_name>). Mismatches produce warnings or errors.
  • RELATIVE mode is more permissive, accepting non-standard directory layouts as long as the namespace prefix can be replaced.

docs.title: Strict Mode Behavior
docs.text:
When storageMigration.strict is true (the default): - Any location mapping issues (mismatched partition paths, paths not aligning with warehouse directories) cause the migration for that table to fail, and all generated SQL is cleared. - This prevents partial or incorrect migrations.

When strict is false: - Issues are logged as warnings, but migration proceeds. - A STORAGE_MIGRATION_NOT_STRICT_ISSUE error is added to alert the user.

docs.title: Configuration Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Default transfer.storageMigration.translationType RELATIVE or ALIGNED. Controls how source paths map to target paths. RELATIVE transfer.storageMigration.dataMovementStrategy SQL or DISTCP. SQL uses Hive INSERT; DISTCP generates Hadoop distcp commands. SQL transfer.storageMigration.dataFlow PULL or PUSH. Direction of distcp execution (which cluster runs the job). PULL transfer.storageMigration.strict When true, any location issues abort the table migration. true transfer.storageMigration.createArchive When true with DISTCP, creates archive table preserving original data. false transfer.storageMigration.consolidateTablesForDistcp When true, consolidates distcp commands across tables in the same database. false transfer.storageMigration.skipDatabaseLocationAdjustments When true, database LOCATION/MANAGEDLOCATION properties are not updated. false transfer.storageMigrationPostfix Suffix appended to the archive table name. _storage_migration transfer.warehouse.external Target warehouse directory for external tables. (required) transfer.warehouse.managed Target warehouse directory for managed tables. (required) migrateACID.on Include ACID tables in the storage migration. false migrateACID.downgrade Convert ACID tables to EXTERNAL+PURGE during migration. false migrateACID.partitionLimit Maximum partitions for ACID tables (safety valve). (configurable) optimization.overrides Session-level Hive property overrides applied during data movement SQL. (none) optimization.sortDynamicPartitionInserts Use sorted dynamic partition inserts for better write performance. false icebergConversion.enable Convert target tables to Iceberg format during migration. false

docs.title: Where This Strategy Is NOT Applicable
docs.text:
• Tables already in the target namespace: Validated at build time. If the table location already starts with the target namespace + warehouse directory, the migration is skipped with a "Table has already been migrated" message.
  • Cross-cluster migrations where both clusters need separate copies: Use SQL, EXPORT_IMPORT, or HYBRID instead.
  • Schema-only changes: Use SCHEMA_ONLY.
  • Views: Views are metadata-only objects with no storage to migrate.
  • Distcp with non-standard partition layouts: When partition directory names do not match partition spec values (e.g., /table/odd/ for partition=1), distcp mode will fail in strict mode or produce warnings.

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
1. HDFS to S3 migration -- Move data from on-premises HDFS to cloud object storage while keeping the same Hive metastore. Use ALIGNED translation to reorganize into standard warehouse layout, or RELATIVE to preserve the existing directory structure.

  2. Namespace consolidation -- Multiple HDFS namespaces merged into one after cluster consolidation. RELATIVE translation replaces only the hdfs://old-nameservice prefix with hdfs://new-nameservice.

  3. Warehouse restructuring -- Reorganize from a flat layout to the Hive 3 aligned warehouse structure with separate external (/warehouse/tablespace/external/hive) and managed (/warehouse/tablespace/managed/hive) directories. ALIGNED translation ensures correct placement.

  4. Ozone migration -- Move from HDFS to Apache Ozone storage within the same cluster. The namespace changes from hdfs:// to ofs:// or o3fs://.

  5. Cloud-to-cloud migration -- Move from one S3 bucket to another (region change, account change). Both SQL and distcp modes work; distcp is preferred for large datasets to avoid serialization overhead.

  6. Distcp-based large table migration -- For tables too large for Hive SQL INSERT (multi-terabyte), enable distcp mode to generate distcp plans. The generated ALTER TABLE SET LOCATION commands update the metadata after external distcp execution completes.

docs.title: (a) SQL-Based Storage Migration (Non-Partitioned Table)
docs.text:
Rename the original table, create a new table at the target location, transfer data via SQL, then drop the archive.

USE sales_db;

-- Rename original table to archive name
ALTER TABLE orders RENAME TO orders_storage_migration;

-- Create new table at target location
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS orders (
    order_id    BIGINT,
    customer_id BIGINT,
    order_date  STRING,
    amount      DECIMAL(10,2)
)
STORED AS ORC
LOCATION 's3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/sales_db.db/orders'
TBLPROPERTIES (
    'HMS_STORAGE_MIGRATION_FLAG'='2026-02-16 10:00:00',
    'EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE'='true'
);

-- Transfer data from archive to new table
FROM orders_storage_migration INSERT OVERWRITE TABLE orders SELECT *;

-- Cleanup: Drop archive table (generated as cleanup SQL)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS orders_storage_migration;

docs.title: (b) Distcp-Based Storage Migration (ALTER TABLE SET LOCATION)
docs.text:
When distcp mode is enabled without archive, the table and partition locations are updated in-place. Data is moved externally via distcp.

USE sales_db;

-- Update table location to new storage namespace
ALTER TABLE orders SET LOCATION "s3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/sales_db.db/orders";

-- Distcp commands generated separately (executed outside Hive):
-- hadoop distcp hdfs://source-cluster/warehouse/sales_db.db/orders s3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/sales_db.db/orders

docs.title: (c) ACID Table Storage Migration (SQL Mode with Downgrade)
docs.text:
ACID tables have their location stripped (Hive 3 manages ACID locations). With downgrade enabled, the table is converted to EXTERNAL+PURGE.

USE finance_db;

-- Rename original ACID table
ALTER TABLE transactions RENAME TO transactions_storage_migration;

-- Create new table (downgraded from ACID to EXTERNAL+PURGE)
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transactions (
    txn_id      BIGINT,
    account_id  BIGINT,
    amount      DECIMAL(10,2),
    txn_date    STRING
)
STORED AS ORC
LOCATION 's3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/finance_db.db/transactions'
TBLPROPERTIES (
    'HMS_STORAGE_MIGRATION_FLAG'='2026-02-16 10:00:00',
    'EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE'='true',
    'DOWNGRADED_FROM_ACID'='true'
);

-- Transfer data
SET hive.exec.dynamic.partition.mode=nonstrict;
FROM transactions_storage_migration INSERT OVERWRITE TABLE transactions SELECT *;

-- Cleanup
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS transactions_storage_migration;

docs.title: (d) Partitioned Table with Individual Partition Location Updates (Distcp Mode)
docs.text:
Each partition's location is individually translated and updated. This handles cases where partitions may have non-standard locations.

USE analytics_db;

-- Update table-level location
ALTER TABLE events SET LOCATION "s3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/analytics_db.db/events";

-- Update each partition's location individually
ALTER TABLE events PARTITION (year='2023', month='01')
    SET LOCATION "s3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/analytics_db.db/events/year=2023/month=01";
ALTER TABLE events PARTITION (year='2023', month='02')
    SET LOCATION "s3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/analytics_db.db/events/year=2023/month=02";
ALTER TABLE events PARTITION (year='2024', month='01')
    SET LOCATION "s3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/analytics_db.db/events/year=2024/month=01";
ALTER TABLE events PARTITION (year='2024', month='02')
    SET LOCATION "s3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/analytics_db.db/events/year=2024/month=02";

-- Distcp commands generated separately (one per unique source directory level):
-- hadoop distcp hdfs://source-cluster/warehouse/analytics_db.db/events s3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/analytics_db.db/events

docs.title: (e) SQL-Based Storage Migration (Partitioned Table with Dynamic Partitions)
docs.text:
For partitioned tables in SQL mode, dynamic partitioning is used to transfer data efficiently.

USE analytics_db;

-- Rename original table
ALTER TABLE events RENAME TO events_storage_migration;

-- Create new table at target location
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (
    event_id    BIGINT,
    user_id     BIGINT,
    event_type  STRING,
    payload     STRING
)
PARTITIONED BY (year STRING, month STRING)
STORED AS ORC
LOCATION 's3a://data-lake/warehouse/external/analytics_db.db/events'
TBLPROPERTIES (
    'HMS_STORAGE_MIGRATION_FLAG'='2026-02-16 10:00:00',
    'EXTERNAL_TABLE_PURGE'='true',
    'DISCOVER_PARTITIONS'='true'
);

-- Set dynamic partition mode
SET hive.optimize.sort.dynamic.partition=true;
SET hive.optimize.sort.dynamic.partition.threshold=0;

-- Transfer data with dynamic partitioning (declarative)
FROM events_storage_migration
INSERT OVERWRITE TABLE events PARTITION (year, month)
SELECT *;

-- Cleanup
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS events_storage_migration;

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Converts tables previously created by the LINKED strategy (pointing to source data) into SCHEMA_ONLY-style tables (pointing to the target namespace). This is the second phase of a two-step migration: LINKED first for immediate access, then CONVERT_LINKED after data has been copied externally (e.g., via distcp).
