Periodically clean up old backup files once you've confirmed the new password is working. Each backup contains the same encryption keys, just protected by a different password.

docs.title: Recovery
docs.text:
If you lose the current master password:

  1. Check for backups in ~/.hms-mirror/security/ (or ~/.hms-mirror/keystore/ in older versions)
  2. If you have a backup and remember that backup's password:
    • Stop HMS-Mirror
    • Copy the backup over the current keystore: cp hms-mirror-keys_20250115_143022.p12 hms-mirror-keys.p12
    • Set the environment variable to the backup's password
    • Start HMS-Mirror
  3. If no backup or previous password is available:
    • Delete the keystore file
    • Restart HMS-Mirror (a new keystore will be created)
    • Re-enter all stored credentials (connection passwords, keytabs, etc.)

Warning

Deleting the keystore file (hms-mirror-keys.p12) permanently destroys all encryption keys. Any data encrypted with those keys — passwords, keytabs, configuration files — becomes unrecoverable. You will need to reconfigure all connections and re-upload all keytabs.

docs.title: Security Considerations
docs.text:
• Override the default — The default development password (hms-mirror-dev-keystore-password) is published in the source code. Always set HMS_MIRROR_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD for any non-development deployment.
  • Use a strong password — Minimum 8 characters with mixed case, numbers, and symbols
  • Use a secrets manager — Store the password in your organization's secrets management solution (HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, etc.) rather than in plain-text scripts
  • File permissions — The keystore file is automatically set to owner-read-only (0400) and the directory to owner-only (0700)
  • Compliance — The encryption key separation architecture complies with PCI-DSS 3.5.2 and NIST SP 800-57
  • Rotate periodically — Follow your organization's password rotation policy by using the change process documented above

docs.title: Preferences
docs.text: Configure HMS-Mirror behavior, security settings, and runtime options through the Preferences section. These settings affect how HMS-Mirror connects to clusters, processes migrations, and integrates with external services.

docs.title: Accessing Preferences
docs.text: Navigate to Settings in the HMS-Mirror web UI to access preference categories.

docs.title: Security
docs.text: Configure authentication and secure communications:

docs.title: Kerberos
docs.text: Enterprise authentication setup: - Configuring krb5.conf paths - Keytab file management - Principal configuration - Ticket renewal and validation - Troubleshooting Kerberos issues

docs.title: Certificates
docs.text: SSL/TLS certificate management: - Managing truststore and keystore - Importing cluster certificates - Self-signed certificate handling - Certificate validation settings

docs.title: Runtime Configurations
docs.text: Application behavior and performance settings:

docs.title: JVM Settings
docs.text: Java Virtual Machine configuration: - Memory allocation (heap size) - Garbage collection tuning - Performance optimization flags - Debugging options

docs.title: LLM Integration
docs.text: AI-powered analysis features: - Supported LLM providers (Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) - Embedded vs. external LLM setup - Model selection and configuration - Privacy considerations - Enabling/disabling AI features

docs.title: Quick Settings Reference
docs.text: Setting Location Purpose Kerberos principal Security → Kerberos Authentication identity Truststore path Security → Certificates SSL certificate validation JVM heap size Runtime → JVM Memory allocation LLM provider Runtime → LLM AI analysis backend

docs.title: Enable Kerberos Authentication
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Security → Kerberos
  2. Configure krb5.conf location
  3. Set principal and keytab path
  4. Test authentication with klist

docs.title: Add Cluster Certificates
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Security → Certificates
  2. Import certificates from clusters
  3. Build combined truststore
  4. Configure connections to use truststore

docs.title: Configure LLM Analysis
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Runtime → LLM
  2. Choose provider (embedded Ollama recommended for privacy)
  3. Select model appropriate for your needs
  4. Enable AI features in the UI

docs.title: Increase Memory for Large Migrations
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Runtime → JVM
  2. Increase -Xmx setting (e.g., -Xmx8g)
  3. Restart HMS-Mirror server
  4. Verify memory allocation in status page

docs.title: Development Environment
docs.text:
- Kerberos: May be disabled
- SSL: Self-signed certificates OK
- LLM: Local Ollama or disabled
- Memory: Default (2-4GB)

docs.title: Production Environment
docs.text:
- Kerberos: Fully configured
- SSL: Proper CA certificates
- LLM: Per security policy
- Memory: Increased (8GB+)

docs.title: Settings Persistence
docs.text:
All preferences are stored in HMS-Mirror's RocksDB database at:

~/.hms-mirror/data/

Settings persist across server restarts. To reset to defaults, clear the RocksDB directory (backup first!).

docs.title: Exporting and Importing Settings
docs.text:
Individual configurations can be exported for backup or sharing:

  1. Navigate to the relevant settings page
  2. Click Export to download as JSON/YAML
  3. Use Import to restore on another instance

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Security - Configure authentication
  • Runtime Configurations - Tune performance
  • Troubleshooting - Fix configuration issues

docs.title: Runtime Configurations
docs.text: Start typing here...

docs.title: Security
docs.text: This page covers JWT session management and the encryption architecture used to protect sensitive data in HMS-Mirror.

docs.title: JWT Secret Management
docs.text: HMS-Mirror uses JWT (JSON Web Tokens) for session authentication. Configuring a persistent JWT secret ensures that user sessions survive application restarts.

docs.title: Default Behavior
docs.text: When no JWT secret is configured (or the configured value is shorter than 32 bytes), HMS-Mirror automatically generates a random secret at startup and logs a warning. The application functions normally, but all sessions are lost on each restart.

docs.title: Setting a Persistent JWT Secret
docs.text: There are several ways to configure a persistent JWT secret, listed in order of priority (highest first):

docs.title: Method 1: Environment Variable (Highest Priority)
docs.text:
Set the HMS_MIRROR_JWT_SECRET environment variable:

export HMS_MIRROR_JWT_SECRET="<your-secret>"

Generate a suitable secret:

openssl rand -base64 48

This method is recommended for Docker and Cloudera Manager deployments.

docs.title: Method 2: Admin UI
docs.text:
Navigate to Preferences > Security in the HMS-Mirror web UI (requires Admin role):

  • Generate & Save: Click to auto-generate a cryptographically secure 48-byte secret. The secret is saved to web.properties and takes effect after restart.
  • Custom Secret: Enter your own secret (minimum 32 characters) and click "Save Secret".

The UI shows whether a JWT secret is currently configured. The actual secret value is never displayed.

docs.title: Method 3: web.properties File
docs.text:
Add the jwt.secret property to ~/.hms-mirror/settings/web.properties:

jwt.secret=<your-base64-secret-at-least-32-bytes>

docs.title: Method 4: Docker
docs.text:
# docker-compose.yml
environment:
  - HMS_MIRROR_JWT_SECRET=<your-secret>

docs.title: Configuration Priority
docs.text:
When multiple methods are used, the highest priority wins:

Priority Source Notes 1 (highest) HMS_MIRROR_JWT_SECRET env var Overrides all other sources 2 web.properties (jwt.secret=...) Set via Admin UI or manually 3 Default (empty) Triggers random generation with warning

docs.title: What Happens When the Secret Changes
docs.text:
When the JWT secret changes between restarts (or when switching from a random to a persistent secret), all existing user sessions become invalid. Users are automatically redirected to the login page and must log in again with their credentials.

When the secret stays the same across restarts, user sessions continue seamlessly.

docs.title: Minimum Secret Length
docs.text: The JWT secret must be at least 32 bytes (characters) to meet the HS256 signing algorithm requirements. Secrets shorter than this are treated as unconfigured, and a random secret is generated instead.

docs.title: Token Lifetimes
docs.text: Token Default Lifetime Purpose Access token 15 minutes Short-lived token for API requests Refresh token 7 days Used to obtain new access tokens without re-login

docs.title: Security Notes
docs.text:
• The JWT secret value is never returned in any API response
  • The web.properties file stores the secret in plaintext; ensure appropriate file permissions
  • The environment variable (HMS_MIRROR_JWT_SECRET) always takes the highest priority
  • Random secret generation uses java.security.SecureRandom with 48 bytes (384 bits)

docs.title: Encryption Architecture
docs.text: HMS-Mirror implements enterprise-grade security controls to protect sensitive data including database credentials, Kerberos keytabs, and configuration files. This section details the cryptographic implementation, compliance standards, and security best practices employed throughout the application.

docs.title: Compliance Standards
docs.text:
The CryptoService is designed to meet or exceed the following industry standards:

Standard Description Requirement Met PCI-DSS Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard AES-256 encryption, secure key management HIPAA Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act Encryption at rest, access controls GDPR General Data Protection Regulation Data protection by design, encryption SOC 2 Service Organization Control Encryption, integrity verification NIST National Institute of Standards and Technology AES-GCM, PBKDF2 with 65,536 iterations

docs.title: AES-256-GCM (Galois/Counter Mode)
docs.text:
HMS-Mirror uses AES-256-GCM for all encryption operations. This authenticated encryption mode provides:

  • Confidentiality - Data is encrypted and unreadable without the key
  • Integrity - Any tampering with ciphertext is detected via authentication tag
  • Authenticity - Verification that data was encrypted with the expected key

docs.title: Algorithm Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Value Purpose Algorithm AES/GCM/NoPadding Authenticated encryption Key Size 256 bits Maximum AES strength GCM Tag Length 128 bits Authentication/integrity verification IV (Nonce) 96 bits (12 bytes) NIST-recommended for GCM PBKDF2 Iterations 65,536 Key derivation from passwords Salt Length 32 bytes Unique per encryption operation

docs.title: Why AES-GCM?
docs.text:
AES-GCM is the recommended mode for authenticated encryption because:

  1. Single-pass encryption - Encryption and authentication in one operation
  2. Parallelizable - High performance on modern hardware
  3. NIST approved - Recommended by NIST SP 800-38D
  4. Wide adoption - Used by TLS 1.3, IPsec, and SSH

docs.title: Encrypted Data Types
docs.text: HMS-Mirror encrypts the following sensitive data types:

docs.title: 1. Kerberos Keytabs
docs.text:
Service: KeytabStorageService

Kerberos keytab files contain cryptographic keys for service authentication. These are binary files that must be protected with the highest security.

Aspect Implementation Storage RocksDB with dedicated column family Encryption AES-256-GCM with master key Key Management Auto-generated master key stored in preferences Integrity SHA-256 hash stored with metadata At Rest Always encrypted in RocksDB In Transit Decrypted only when needed for authentication

Storage Format: 

RocksDB Key: data:{keytabId}
RocksDB Value: IV (12 bytes) || Ciphertext || GCM Tag (16 bytes)

docs.title: 2. Connection Passwords
docs.text:
Service: ConnectionPasswordService

Database connection passwords for HiveServer2, Metastore Direct, and other JDBC connections.

Aspect Implementation Storage Encrypted in ConnectionDto before persistence Marker Encrypted passwords prefixed with ENC: Encryption AES-256-GCM with dedicated master key Decryption On-demand when establishing connections

Storage Format: 

ENC:{Base64(IV || Ciphertext || Tag)}

docs.title: 3. Kerberos Credential Passwords
docs.text:
Service: KerberosCredentialPasswordService

Passwords for password-based Kerberos authentication (alternative to keytabs).

Aspect Implementation Storage Encrypted in KerberosCredential objects Encryption AES-256-GCM with dedicated master key Use Case Password-based Kerberos (non-keytab) authentication

docs.title: 4. Trino Connection Passwords
docs.text:
Service: TrinoConnectionPasswordService

Credentials for Trino/Presto SQL connections used for metadata discovery.

Aspect Implementation Storage Encrypted in TrinoConnectionDto Encryption AES-256-GCM with dedicated master key

docs.title: 5. HCFS Configuration Files
docs.text:
Service: HcfsConfigFileStorageService

Hadoop configuration files (core-site.xml, hdfs-site.xml, etc.) that may contain sensitive properties.

Aspect Implementation Storage Encrypted ZIP archives in RocksDB Allowed Files core-site.xml, hdfs-site.xml, yarn-site.xml, hive-site.xml, etc. Encryption AES-256-GCM with dedicated master key Extraction To temp directory with restrictive permissions

docs.title: 6. Kerberos Preferences
docs.text:
Service: KerberosPreferencesService

Legacy global Kerberos configuration including keytab paths and passwords.

Aspect Implementation Storage Encrypted in RocksDB preferences Encryption AES-256-GCM with dedicated master key

docs.title: Key Separation (v4.2+)
docs.text:
HMS-Mirror implements secure key separation by storing encryption keys in a PKCS12 keystore, completely separate from the encrypted data stored in RocksDB. This architecture complies with:

  • PCI-DSS 3.5.2: Cryptographic keys must be stored separately from encrypted data
  • NIST SP 800-57: Key management best practices
  • Defense in Depth: Compromising the database does not expose encryption keys

docs.title: Keystore Architecture
docs.text:
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                    PKCS12 Keystore (hms-mirror-keys.p12)              |
|                    Location: ~/.hms-mirror/keystore/                   |
|                    Password: External (env var or system property)     |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  connections_key           -> AES-256 Master Key                      |
|  keytabs_key               -> AES-256 Master Key                      |
|  kerberos_credentials_key  -> AES-256 Master Key                      |
|  trino_connections_key     -> AES-256 Master Key                      |
|  hcfs_config_files_key     -> AES-256 Master Key                      |
|  kerberos_preferences_key  -> AES-256 Master Key                      |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
                                    |
                          Password Required to Access
                                    |
                +---------------------------------------------+
                |         Password Source Options              |
                |  1. HMS_MIRROR_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD (env)       |
                |  2. -Dhms-mirror.keystore.password            |
                |  3. Default dev password (application.yaml)  |
                +---------------------------------------------+

docs.title: Key Generation Process
docs.text:
Keys are:

  • Generated using java.security.SecureRandom
  • 256 bits in length
  • Stored in PKCS12 keystore (password-protected)
  • Generated once per domain and reused

docs.title: Providing the Keystore Password
docs.text:
For production deployments, you must provide the keystore password externally:

Option 1: Environment Variable (Recommended) 

export HMS_MIRROR_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD="your-secure-password-here"
java -jar hmsm-server-*.jar

Option 2: System Property 

java -Dhms-mirror.keystore.password="your-secure-password-here" -jar hmsm-server-*.jar

Option 3: Default Development Password If no environment variable or system property is set, HMS-Mirror uses the default development password defined in application.yaml (hms-mirror-dev-keystore-password). This must be overridden for production deployments since it is a well-known value.

docs.title: Migration from Previous Versions
docs.text:
HMS-Mirror v4.2+ automatically migrates encryption keys from RocksDB to the keystore on first startup. After migration:

  • Keys are removed from RocksDB
  • All future encryption operations use the keystore
  • Existing encrypted data remains unchanged (same keys, different storage location)

docs.title: Keystore File Permissions
docs.text:
The keystore directory and file are created with restrictive POSIX permissions to prevent unauthorized access:

Path Permission Octal Purpose ~/.hms-mirror/keystore/ rwx------ 700 Directory requires execute for file access hms-mirror-keys.p12 r-------- 400 Read-only prevents tampering with keys

Why these permissions?

  • Directory (700): Owner needs read/write/execute. Execute permission on directories allows accessing files inside. Without it, even the owner cannot create or read files.
  • Keystore file (400): Read-only after creation prevents accidental modification or tampering. The file is temporarily made writable (600) only during key rotation operations.

Troubleshooting Permission Issues

If you encounter AccessDeniedException when starting HMS-Mirror:

# Fix directory permissions
chmod 700 ~/.hms-mirror/keystore

# If keystore file is corrupted or has wrong permissions, remove it
# (keys will be regenerated, but existing encrypted data will be unrecoverable)
rm ~/.hms-mirror/keystore/hms-mirror-keys.p12

Warning

Deleting the keystore file means losing all encryption keys. Any data encrypted with those keys (passwords, keytabs, config files) will become permanently unrecoverable.

docs.title: Password-Based Key Derivation
docs.text:
For password-encrypted data, keys are derived using PBKDF2 with:

  • Algorithm: PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256
  • Salt: 32 random bytes (unique per encryption operation)
  • Iterations: 65,536 (NIST-recommended)
  • Output key size: 256 bits

docs.title: Temporary File Security
docs.text: When keytabs or configuration files are extracted for use, strict security controls are applied:

docs.title: File Permissions
docs.text: Permission Value Description Owner Read Yes Only the process owner can read Owner Write No Immutable after creation Group None No group access Other None No world access

docs.title: Temporary File Lifecycle
docs.text:
1. Decrypt — Data is decrypted from RocksDB into memory
  2. Write — Written to temp file with POSIX permissions 0400 (owner read-only)
  3. Use — File used for authentication (e.g., Kerberos kinit)
  4. Cleanup — File deleted when session closes or application stops

docs.title: Cleanup Mechanisms
docs.text:
1. Session-based cleanup - Temp files deleted when session closes
  2. Service shutdown - All temp files cleaned on application stop
  3. Explicit cleanup - cleanupTempFiles() method available
  4. Tracking - All temp file paths tracked for guaranteed cleanup

docs.title: SHA-256 Content Hash
docs.text:
Keytabs and configuration files are hashed on upload. This enables:

  • Detection of storage corruption
  • Verification of data authenticity
  • Duplicate detection

docs.title: GCM Authentication Tag
docs.text:
The AES-GCM mode includes a 128-bit authentication tag that:

  • Verifies ciphertext integrity
  • Detects any bit flips or tampering
  • Automatically validated during decryption
  • Throws exception if verification fails

docs.title: Encryption Marker System
docs.text:
Encrypted passwords use a marker prefix to distinguish them from plaintext:

Original:    MySecretPassword123
Encrypted:   ENC:dGhpcyBpcyBhIGJhc2U2NCBlbmNvZGVkIGVuY3J5cHRlZCB2YWx1ZQ==

This enables:

  • Safe re-encryption detection (don't double-encrypt)
  • Clear identification of encrypted values in logs/debugging
  • Backward compatibility with unencrypted legacy data

docs.title: What HMS-Mirror Does
docs.text:
1. Never logs sensitive data - Passwords and keys are never written to logs
  2. Memory cleanup - Sensitive data cleared after use where possible
  3. Minimal exposure - Decryption only when needed, not preemptively
  4. Separate keys - Each data type has its own master key
  5. Random IVs - Fresh IV for every encryption operation
  6. Strong RNG - Uses java.security.SecureRandom

docs.title: Recommendations for Operators
docs.text:
1. Secure RocksDB directory - Restrict filesystem permissions to application user
  2. Regular backups - Back up RocksDB directory (data remains encrypted)
  3. Key rotation - Consider periodic key rotation for long-lived deployments
  4. Network security - Use TLS for all network connections
  5. Audit logging - Enable audit logs to track sensitive operations

docs.title: Version History
docs.text: Version Date Changes 4.1.0 2025 Initial CryptoService implementation with AES-256-GCM 4.1.1 2025 Added HCFS config file encryption, keytab storage 4.1.2 2025 Added Kerberos credential password encryption 4.2.0 2025 Key Separation: Moved encryption keys from RocksDB to PKCS12 keystore for PCI-DSS/NIST compliance. Added KeyStoreService, external password support, and automatic key migration.

docs.title: TLS/HTTPS Configuration
docs.text: Configure HTTPS encryption for secure access to the HMS-Mirror web interface. HMS-Mirror can generate self-signed certificates for development or internal deployments.

docs.title: Accessing TLS Settings
docs.text:
Navigate to Preferences > TLS to configure HTTPS settings.

Note

TLS settings are not available when Cloudera Manager mode is enabled.

docs.title: Certificate Generation
docs.text: Before enabling TLS, you must generate a server certificate.

docs.title: Certificate Fields
docs.text: Field Default Description Keystore Password (required) Password protecting the server keystore Key Password (required) Separate password for key protection Server Common Name (CN) hms-mirror-server Certificate subject CN Server Hostname localhost Hostname for the certificate Organization (O) HMS-Mirror Organization name in the certificate Organizational Unit (OU) Data Engineering Organizational unit State/Province (ST) California State or province City/Locality (L) San Francisco City or locality Country (C) US Two-letter country code Validity (days) 365 Certificate validity period (30-3650 days) Key Size 2048 RSA key size in bits (2048 or 4096) TLS Protocol TLSv1.3 Protocol version (TLSv1.2 or TLSv1.3) Additional SANs (optional) Comma-separated Subject Alternative Names

docs.title: Generating a Certificate
docs.text:
1. Fill in the certificate details (or accept the defaults)
  2. Set a strong keystore password
  3. Click Generate Certificate
  4. The certificate status will display with generation date and expiry

docs.title: Certificate Status
docs.text:
Once generated, the TLS tab shows:

  • Certificate Generated status with generation and expiration dates
  • Color-coded expiry indicator (green if valid, red if expiring within 30 days)
  • Delete Certificate button to remove and reset

docs.title: Enabling TLS
docs.text:
1. Generate a certificate (see above)
  2. Check Enable TLS
  3. Click Save Settings
  4. Restart HMS-Mirror for changes to take effect
  5. Access the application via https:// instead of http://

Warning

Self-signed certificates will trigger browser security warnings. For production use, import the certificate into your browser's trusted certificate store or use a certificate signed by your organization's CA.

docs.title: Subject Alternative Names (SANs)
docs.text:
If users will access HMS-Mirror using multiple hostnames or IP addresses, add them as SANs:

192.168.1.100, myserver.example.com, hms-mirror.internal

This prevents certificate mismatch errors when accessing the server from different network paths.

docs.title: HMS-Mirror Configuration Validation Rules
docs.text: This document describes the validation rules that HMS-Mirror enforces before executing a migration job. These rules ensure that the combination of data strategy, configuration options, connection settings, and platform types form a valid and safe migration plan.

docs.title: Concepts
docs.text:
• LEFT: The source cluster/environment
  • RIGHT: The target cluster/environment
  • Data Strategy: The migration approach (SCHEMA_ONLY, SQL, EXPORT_IMPORT, HYBRID, LINKED, STORAGE_MIGRATION, DUMP, COMMON, CONVERT_LINKED, ICEBERG_CONVERSION)
  • ACID tables: Hive transactional tables (managed tables in Hive 3+)
  • Legacy Hive: Hive 1.x/2.x (pre-transactional)
  • distcp: Hadoop Distributed Copy, used for large-scale data movement
  • Warehouse Plan: Defines external and managed warehouse directory mappings for a database
  • HCFS Profile: Hadoop-Compatible File System profile defining namespace and storage configuration
  • Target Namespace: The root filesystem namespace (e.g., hdfs://namenode, s3a://bucket, ofs://vol) for the migration target

docs.title: 1.1 LEFT Connection Required
docs.text:
• Rule: A LEFT (source) connection must always be defined.
  • Error: MISSING_CONFIGURATION: LEFT(Source) connection

docs.title: 1.2 RIGHT Connection Conditionally Required
docs.text:
• Rule: A RIGHT (target) connection is required UNLESS:
  • The strategy is STORAGE_MIGRATION or DUMP
  • OR ACID in-place downgrade is enabled (migrateACID.inplace=true AND migrateACID.downgrade=true) with strategy SQL, EXPORT_IMPORT, or HYBRID
  • Error: MISSING_CONFIGURATION: RIGHT(Target) connection

docs.title: 1.3 Dataset Required
docs.text:
• Rule: A dataset must be defined with at least one database specified.
  • Error: MISSING_CONFIGURATION: Dataset or MISSING_CONFIGURATION: Databases

docs.title: 2.1 HiveServer2 Driver
docs.text:
• Rule: When a connection has an HS2 URI configured, the corresponding Hive JDBC driver must be available on the classpath.
  • Applies to: Both LEFT and RIGHT connections.
  • Error: DRIVER_NOT_FOUND: <environment>: HiveServer2 driver not found: <driver>. Please download via Utilities -> Drivers -> Hive Drivers

docs.title: 2.2 Metastore Direct Driver
docs.text:
• Rule: When a connection has Metastore Direct enabled with a URI configured, the corresponding RDBMS JDBC driver (MySQL, MariaDB, Postgres, or Oracle) must be installed.
  • Applies to: Both LEFT and RIGHT connections.
  • Error: DRIVER_NOT_FOUND: <environment>: Metastore JDBC driver not installed: <type>. Please install via Utilities -> Drivers -> Metastore RDBMS Drivers

docs.title: 3.1 Target Namespace Profile
docs.text:
• Rule: A target namespace must be resolvable from an HCFS profile. Resolution order:
  • Explicit targetNamespaceProfileKey on the job
  • RIGHT connection's hcfsConfigProfileKey (for most strategies)
  • Exception: LINKED strategy uses LEFT connection's namespace; no target namespace needed.
  • Error: TARGET_NAMESPACE_PROFILE_REQUIRED

docs.title: 3.2 HCFS Profile Must Exist with Namespace
docs.text:
• Rule: When an HCFS profile key is referenced, the profile must exist and must have a defaultNamespace configured.
  • Errors: HCFS_PROFILE_NOT_FOUND: <profileKey> or HCFS_PROFILE_MISSING_NAMESPACE: <profileKey>

docs.title: 3.3 Intermediate Storage Profile
docs.text:
• Rule: When an intermediate storage profile key is specified on the job, the referenced profile must exist.
  • Error: HCFS_PROFILE_NOT_FOUND: <profileKey>

docs.title: 4.1 No Hive Downgrades
docs.text:
• Rule: The RIGHT cluster's Hive version must be greater than or equal to the LEFT cluster's Hive version. Migrating from Hive 3 to Hive 1/2 is not supported.
  • Error: HIVE_DOWNGRADE_REQUESTED

docs.title: 4.2 Non-Legacy to Legacy Prohibited
docs.text:
• Rule: Migrating from a non-legacy Hive (Hive 3+) source to a legacy Hive (Hive 1/2) target is not supported.
  • Exception: Does not apply when ACID in-place downgrade is enabled (single-cluster operation).
  • Error: NON_LEGACY_TO_LEGACY

docs.title: 4.3 Legacy Hive Cannot Be CONVERT_LINKED Target
docs.text:
• Rule: The CONVERT_LINKED strategy does not support legacy Hive as the RIGHT (target) cluster.
  • Error: LEGACY_HIVE_RIGHT_CLUSTER

docs.title: 5.1 ACID is Not a Top-Level Strategy
docs.text:
• Rule: The ACID strategy cannot be selected directly. Use HYBRID or SQL with the migrateACID option enabled.
  • Error: ACID_NOT_TOP_LEVEL_STRATEGY

docs.title: 5.2 DUMP Strategy
docs.text: • Rule: Translation type is forced to RELATIVE. Data movement strategy is forced to NA. Execution is forced to dry-run mode.

docs.title: 5.3 STORAGE_MIGRATION Strategy
docs.text:
• Rule: Not available for Legacy Hive sources.

  • Error: STORAGE_MIGRATION_NOT_AVAILABLE_FOR_LEGACY

  • Rule: The LEFT connection must have Metastore Direct configured (required for pulling table/partition location details).

  • Error: METASTORE_DIRECT_NOT_DEFINED_OR_CONFIGURED: LEFT

  • Rule: Translation type is forced to ALIGNED.

  • Rule: When source platform is HDP3, forceExternalLocation is automatically set to true, and ACID transfers without distcp are prohibited.

  • Error: HIVE3_ON_HDP_ACID_TRANSFERS

  • Rule: Cannot use distcp with ACID tables when createArchive is also enabled.

  • Error: STORAGE_MIGRATION_ACID_ARCHIVE_DISTCP

docs.title: 5.4 SCHEMA_ONLY Strategy
docs.text:
• Rule: ACID downgrade is not supported with SCHEMA_ONLY (requires SQL transformations).

  • Error: ACID_DOWNGRADE_SCHEMA_ONLY

  • Rule: With RELATIVE translation type, data movement strategy is forced to MANUAL (not SQL).

  • Rule: With ALIGNED translation type, data movement strategy is forced to DISTCP.

  • Warning (DISTCP_W_RELATIVE): Using distcp with RELATIVE translations only inspects database-level locations, not individual table/partition locations.

  • Warning (RELATIVE_MANUAL): RELATIVE with MANUAL may not correctly migrate non-standard table/partition locations.

docs.title: 5.5 SQL and HYBRID Strategies
docs.text:
• Rule: Data movement strategy is forced to SQL.
  • Rule: distcp with ACID tables is only valid when downgrading (migrateACID.downgrade=true).
  • Error: SQL_DISTCP_ONLY_W_DA_ACID

docs.title: 5.6 EXPORT_IMPORT Strategy
docs.text: • Rule: Data movement strategy is forced to NA.

docs.title: 5.7 LINKED Strategy
docs.text:
• Rule: ACID table migration is not supported with LINKED (cannot link transactional tables).

  • Error: LINKED_NO_ACID_SUPPORT

  • Rule: Intermediate storage is not valid with LINKED.

  • Error: INTERMEDIATE_STORAGE_WITH_LINKED

  • Rule: Target namespace/HCFS profile is removed (LINKED uses LEFT namespace).

docs.title: 5.8 HDP3 Target Warning
docs.text:
• Rule: When the RIGHT cluster is HDP with Hive 3, forceExternalLocation is automatically set to true.
  • Warning: HDP3_HIVE

docs.title: 6.1 Valid ACID Strategies
docs.text:
• Rule: ACID migration (migrateACID.on=true) is only valid with: SCHEMA_ONLY, DUMP, SQL, EXPORT_IMPORT, HYBRID, STORAGE_MIGRATION.
  • Error: VALID_ACID_STRATEGIES

docs.title: 6.2 ACID In-Place Downgrade
docs.text:
• Rule: In-place ACID downgrade (migrateACID.inplace=true) is only valid with the SQL strategy.

  • Error: VALID_ACID_DA_IP_STRATEGIES

  • Rule: In-place downgrade requires a target namespace to be defined.

  • Error: TARGET_NAMESPACE_NOT_DEFINED

  • Rule: In-place downgrade cannot use intermediate storage.

  • Error: INTERMEDIATE_STORAGE_WITH_DA_IP

  • Rule: In-place downgrade cannot use distcp.

  • Error: DISTCP_W_DA_IP_ACID

  • Rule: In-place downgrade only works on non-legacy Hive (Hive 3+).

  • Error: DA_IP_NON_LEGACY

docs.title: 7.1 Strategy Restriction
docs.text: • Rule: Iceberg conversion is only available with STORAGE_MIGRATION or SQL strategies. If enabled with other strategies, it is automatically disabled.

docs.title: 7.2 Data Movement Restriction
docs.text:
• Rule: Iceberg conversion cannot use distcp. Only SQL data movement is supported.

  • Error: ICEBERG_CONVERSION_DISTCP

  • Rule: When Iceberg conversion is enabled with STORAGE_MIGRATION, data movement strategy is forced to SQL.

docs.title: 8.1 Valid Sync Strategies
docs.text:
• Rule: The sync option is only valid with: SCHEMA_ONLY, LINKED, SQL, EXPORT_IMPORT, HYBRID, COMMON.
  • Error: VALID_SYNC_STRATEGIES

docs.title: 8.2 Sync Behavior
docs.text: • Rule: When sync is enabled with SCHEMA_ONLY or LINKED, disaster recovery mode is automatically enabled.

docs.title: 8.3 Sync with Table Filters
docs.text: • Warning (SYNC_TBL_FILTER): When sync is combined with table filters, synchronization is bi-directional only for tables matching the filter on both sides.

docs.title: 9.1 Valid Read-Only Strategies
docs.text:
• Rule: Read-only mode is only valid with: SCHEMA_ONLY, LINKED, COMMON, SQL.
  • Error: RO_VALID_STRATEGIES

docs.title: 10.1 dbRename and dbPrefix Are Mutually Exclusive
docs.text:
• Rule: You cannot set both dbRename and dbPrefix on the same database specification.
  • Error: CONFLICTING_PROPERTIES: dbRename, dbPrefix

docs.title: 11.1 Aligned Distcp Requires Warehouse Plans
docs.text:
• Rule: When using ALIGNED translation type with distcp data movement, warehouse plans must be defined for the databases.
  • Error: ALIGN_LOCATIONS_WITHOUT_WAREHOUSE_PLANS

docs.title: 11.2 Distcp with Table Filters
docs.text: • Warning (DISTCP_W_TABLE_FILTERS): When using distcp with table filters, separate distcp plans are built per table. This may affect completeness of data transfer.

docs.title: 11.3 SQL ACID Downgrade with Distcp
docs.text:
• Rule: Using SQL strategy with ACID downgrade and distcp requires external warehouse directory (-ewd) to be specified.
  • Error: SQL_ACID_DA_DISTCP_WO_EXT_WAREHOUSE

docs.title: 12.1 Volume Name Length
docs.text:
• Rule: When the target namespace uses ofs:// (Ozone), volume names derived from warehouse directory paths must be at least 3 characters long.
  • Applies to: Both external and managed warehouse directories in each database specification.
  • Error: OZONE_VOLUME_NAME_TOO_SHORT

docs.title: 13.1 Namespace Required for Most Strategies
docs.text:
• Rule: A resolvable target namespace is required for all strategies except DUMP and LINKED.
  • Error: TARGET_NAMESPACE_NOT_DEFINED

docs.title: 14. Configuration Auto-Adjustments
docs.text:
The validation process also makes automatic adjustments to invalid-but-recoverable configuration combinations. These are logged as configuration adjustment messages but do not fail the validation.

Scenario Auto-Adjustment Iceberg conversion enabled with unsupported strategy Iceberg conversion disabled DUMP with non-RELATIVE translation type Translation type set to RELATIVE DUMP with execute enabled Forced to dry-run SCHEMA_ONLY + RELATIVE + SQL data movement Data movement set to MANUAL SCHEMA_ONLY + ALIGNED + non-DISTCP data movement Data movement set to DISTCP SQL/HYBRID with non-SQL data movement Data movement set to SQL EXPORT_IMPORT with non-NA data movement Data movement set to NA STORAGE_MIGRATION with non-ALIGNED translation Translation set to ALIGNED STORAGE_MIGRATION + Iceberg + non-SQL data movement Data movement set to SQL STORAGE_MIGRATION with NA data movement Data movement set to DISTCP LINKED with target namespace defined Target namespace removed Sync with SCHEMA_ONLY or LINKED Disaster recovery mode enabled HDP3 target cluster forceExternalLocation set to true

docs.title: Rule Evaluation Order
docs.text:
1. Required components check — LEFT connection, dataset, databases (fail-fast if missing)
  2. Driver validation — Verify JDBC drivers for both HS2 and Metastore Direct
  3. HCFS profile validation — Target namespace and intermediate storage profiles
  4. Configuration alignment — Auto-adjust recoverable misconfigurations
  5. Strategy-specific validation — Rules specific to the selected data strategy
  6. Cross-cutting validation — Ozone volumes, ACID rules, sync rules, read-only rules
  7. Final error check — Any accumulated errors from sub-validators fail the validation

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: HMS-Mirror provides a comprehensive REST API for programmatic access to all functionality. The API follows RESTful conventions and returns JSON responses.

docs.title: Interactive API Documentation (Swagger)
docs.text:
HMS-Mirror includes built-in interactive API documentation powered by SpringDoc/OpenAPI 3.

Resource URL Swagger UI http://<host>:8090/hms-mirror/swagger-ui.html OpenAPI JSON http://<host>:8090/hms-mirror/v3/api-docs

The Swagger UI provides a complete, interactive reference for every endpoint, including request/response schemas, parameter details, and the ability to execute API calls directly from the browser. It is the authoritative source for request body schemas and response types.

Tip

Use the Swagger UI as your primary API exploration tool. This document provides an overview and quick reference, while Swagger provides full schema details, example payloads, and a "Try it out" feature for live testing.

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text:
http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1

The context path /hms-mirror is configurable via server.servlet.context-path in application.yaml.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: HMS-Mirror uses JWT (JSON Web Token) authentication. All API endpoints except the public auth and health endpoints require a valid access token.

docs.title: Step 1 — Login
docs.text:
POST /api/v1/auth/login
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "usernameOrEmail": "admin",
  "password": "your-password"
}

Note

The field is usernameOrEmail — you can provide either the account username or its registered email address.

Response:

{
  "accessToken": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9...",
  "refreshToken": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9...",
  "tokenType": "Bearer",
  "expiresIn": 900000,
  "user": {
    "id": "...",
    "username": "admin",
    "email": "admin@example.com",
    "roles": ["USER", "ADMIN"]
  }
}

docs.title: Step 2 — Use the access token (standard REST calls)
docs.text:
Include the accessToken from the login response in the Authorization header for all subsequent requests:

Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/connections

docs.title: Step 3 — Use the access token for SSE streams
docs.text:
Two LLM endpoints stream progress via Server-Sent Events (text/event-stream):

  • GET /api/v1/preferences/llm/embedded/download — Ollama binary download progress
  • GET /api/v1/preferences/llm/models/pull — Model pull progress

The browser's native EventSource API cannot set custom HTTP headers, so pass the access token as a ?token= query parameter instead:

GET /api/v1/preferences/llm/models/pull?model=llama3&token=<accessToken>

# cURL equivalent (for testing)
curl -N -H "Accept: text/event-stream" \
  "http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/preferences/llm/models/pull?model=llama3&token=$TOKEN"

Note

Access tokens are short-lived (15 minutes), which limits the exposure window if a ?token= URL appears in server logs. Never use refresh tokens in query parameters.

docs.title: Step 4 — Refresh the access token
docs.text:
Access tokens expire after 15 minutes. Use the refresh token to get a new access token without re-logging in:

POST /api/v1/auth/refresh
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "refreshToken": "<refreshToken>"
}

No Authorization header is required on this call. The response returns a new accessToken (and optionally a new refreshToken).

Refresh tokens are valid for 7 days. After expiry the user must log in again.

docs.title: Token Lifecycle
docs.text: Token Lifetime Purpose accessToken 15 minutes Authenticate all API calls and SSE streams refreshToken 7 days Obtain a new accessToken without re-logging in

docs.title: Role-Based Access
docs.text:
Endpoints are protected by role-based access control:

Role Access USER Connections, jobs, datasets, configurations, reports, self-service profile ADMIN All USER access plus user management, preferences, master password, system configuration

Admin-only endpoints require ROLE_ADMIN and return 403 Forbidden for standard users.

docs.title: Response Format
docs.text:
Successful responses typically use this envelope:

{
  "status": "SUCCESS",
  "data": { ... },
  "message": "Operation completed"
}

Error responses:

{
  "status": "ERROR",
  "message": "Error description"
}

docs.title: API Overview by Functional Area
docs.text:
The table below provides a quick reference to all API controllers. Each section is documented in detail further down this page.

Area Base Path Endpoints Description Authentication /api/v1/auth 7 Login, registration, JWT tokens, auth status OIDC Authentication /api/v1/auth/oidc 2 OpenID Connect SSO configuration User Management /api/v1/users 10 User profiles, admin user creation and operations Connections /api/v1/connections 11 Hive connection CRUD, testing, HCFS browsing Trino Connections /api/v1/trino-connections 6 Trino connection CRUD and testing Configurations /api/v1/config 7 Migration configuration CRUD Datasets /api/v1/datasets 6 Dataset CRUD and validation Jobs /api/v1/jobs 7 Job CRUD, validation, and report retrieval Runtime Jobs /api/v1/runtime/jobs 5 Job execution, status, and monitoring Runtime Reports /api/v1/runtime/reports 7 Conversion results and report details Reports /api/v1/reports 9 Report browsing, details, and downloads Migration Plans /api/v1/plans 14 Multi-database migration orchestration HCFS Profiles /api/v1/hcfs-profiles 13 HDFS/OFS/S3 storage configuration profiles CM Connector /api/v1/connectors/cm 10 Cloudera Manager integration and import Data Browser /api/v1/data-browser 12 SQL execution sessions and script management Discovery /api/v1/connections 2 Database and table discovery from connections Kerberos Credentials /api/v1/kerberos-credentials 9 Kerberos credential and keytab management Certificates /api/v1/certificates 7 TLS certificate and truststore management Driver Management /api/v1/drivers 9 Hive and Trino JDBC driver management Metastore Drivers /api/v1/drivers/metastore 5 Metastore-specific driver management Preferences /api/v1/preferences 34 Kerberos, JVM, TLS, LDAP, Backup, Master Password settings Application Preferences /api/v1/preferences/application 5 UI preferences (dataset page, job page) LLM / AI Advisor /api/v1/preferences/llm 26 LLM configuration, model management, AI analysis System Status /api/v1/system 6 Health checks, JVM/LLM/SSL/RocksDB status Application Info /api/v1/app 4 Version, features, platform types RocksDB Management /api/v1/rocksdb 23 Database health, backup, restore, compaction CLI Import /api/v1/cli-import 3 Import connections from legacy CLI config files Report Import /api/v1/reports/import 2 Import reports from filesystem

docs.title: Authentication API
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/auth

Method Path Description GET /status Get authentication system status (auth type, OIDC/LDAP availability) POST /register Register a new user account POST /login Authenticate and receive JWT tokens POST /refresh Refresh an expired access token GET /me Get current authenticated user details GET /config Get authentication configuration POST /ldap/test Test LDAP connection (ADMIN only)

docs.title: OIDC Authentication
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/auth/oidc

Method Path Description GET /config Get OIDC configuration status and provider details GET /available Check if OIDC authentication is available

docs.title: User Management
docs.text: Base Path: /api/v1/users

docs.title: Self-Service Endpoints
docs.text: Method Path Description GET /me/profile Get current user's profile PUT /me/profile Update current user's profile (email) POST /me/change-password Change current user's password

docs.title: Admin Endpoints (ADMIN role required)
docs.text: Method Path Description POST `` Create a new user account. If password is omitted, a random password is generated and returned in the response — share it with the new user securely. GET `` List all users GET /{userId} Get user details POST /{userId}/reset-password Reset a user's password POST /{userId}/enable Enable a user account POST /{userId}/disable Disable a user account PUT /{userId}/admin Grant or revoke admin role PUT /{userId}/data-browser Grant or revoke DATA_BROWSER role

docs.title: Connections API
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/connections

Method Path Description GET `` List all connections (supports search, environment, status query params) GET /{key} Get connection by key POST `` Create a new connection PUT /{key} Update an existing connection DELETE /{key} Delete a connection POST /{key}/test Test connection connectivity GET /{key}/driver-status Check JDBC driver availability for connection GET /{key}/hcfs/browse Browse HCFS directories (path query param) POST /{key}/hcfs/mkdir Create an HCFS directory

docs.title: Trino Connections
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/trino-connections

Method Path Description GET `` List all Trino connections GET /{key} Get Trino connection by key POST `` Create a Trino connection PUT /{key} Update a Trino connection DELETE /{key} Delete a Trino connection POST /{key}/test Test Trino connection

docs.title: Configurations API
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/config

Method Path Description GET `` List all configurations GET /{configName} Get configuration by name POST `` Create a new configuration PUT /{configName} Update a configuration DELETE /{configName} Delete a configuration POST /copy/{sourceConfigName} Copy a configuration GET /strategies List available data strategies

docs.title: Datasets API
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/datasets

Method Path Description GET `` List all datasets GET /{key} Get dataset by key POST `` Create a new dataset PUT /{key} Update a dataset DELETE /{key} Delete a dataset POST /validate Validate a dataset without saving

docs.title: Jobs API
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/jobs

Method Path Description GET `` List all jobs GET /{jobKey} Get job by key POST /{jobKey} Create or update a job DELETE /{jobKey} Delete a job POST /{jobKey}/validate Validate job configuration GET /{jobKey}/reports Get reports produced by this job GET /reports Get all job reports

docs.title: Runtime Jobs
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/runtime/jobs

Method Path Description GET `` List runtime job statuses (paginated) GET /{key} Get job status by key DELETE /{key} Delete a job status GET /current Get currently running job statuses POST /start Start a job execution

docs.title: Runtime Reports
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/runtime/reports

Method Path Description GET `` List conversion results (paginated) GET ?key= Get conversion result by key DELETE ?key= Delete a conversion result GET /files?key= List available report files GET /file?key=&filename= Download a specific report file GET /details?key= Get detailed report information GET /database-details?key=&database= Get database details with properties and SQL

docs.title: Reports API
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/reports

Method Path Description GET /browse Browse reports directory (path query param) GET /details Get report details (path query param) GET /artifacts Get artifacts for a report (path query param) GET /table-details Get table details (path, table, environment query params) GET /database-details Get database details (path query param) GET /file Get a specific report file (path, file query params) GET /artifacts/download Download an artifact (path query param) GET /download-by-key Download report by ConversionResult key as zip GET /download-all Download all artifacts as zip (path query param)

docs.title: Migration Plans
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/plans

Migration plans orchestrate multi-database migrations with priority ordering and bulk operations.

Method Path Description GET `` List all migration plans GET /{key} Get a plan by key POST `` Create a new migration plan PUT /{key} Update a migration plan DELETE /{key} Delete a migration plan PUT /{key}/databases/{databaseName} Update database priority (priority query param) PUT /{key}/databases/bulk-priority Bulk update database priorities GET /{key}/progress Get migration progress summary GET /by-connection/{connectionKey} Get plan by connection key POST /{key}/datasets Create a Dataset from selected databases PUT /{key}/databases/{databaseName}/configurations Assign configurations to a database PUT /{key}/databases/bulk-configurations Bulk assign configurations to databases POST /{key}/jobs Create jobs from selected databases POST /{key}/jobs/{jobKey}/start Launch a job from a plan

docs.title: HCFS Profiles
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/hcfs-profiles

HCFS (Hadoop Compatible File System) profiles store HDFS, OFS, or S3 storage configurations including namespaces and warehouse paths.

Method Path Description GET `` List all HCFS configuration profiles GET /{key} Get a profile by key POST `` Create a profile PUT /{key} Update a profile DELETE /{key} Delete a profile POST /{key}/test Test a profile POST /{key}/upload-file Upload a config file (multipart) POST /{key}/upload-archive Upload a config archive (multipart) GET /{key}/files List config files in a profile DELETE /{key}/files/{filename} Remove a config file POST /{key}/test-save Test profile save/load cycle GET /{key}/browse Browse HCFS directory GET /{key}/extract-namespaces Extract namespace options from config files

docs.title: Cloudera Manager Connector
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/connectors/cm

Import Hive connections and HCFS profiles directly from Cloudera Manager instances.

Method Path Description GET `` List all CM connections GET /{key} Get a CM connection by key POST `` Create a CM connection PUT /{key} Update a CM connection DELETE /{key} Delete a CM connection POST /{key}/test Test CM connection GET /{key}/clusters Discover clusters from CM GET /{key}/discover Discover importable items (HS2, HCFS profiles) POST /{key}/import-items Import selected items from CM POST /{key}/import Import clusters from CM

Note

CM Connector endpoints are only available when the hms-mirror.features.cm.enabled feature flag is active.

docs.title: Data Browser
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/data-browser

Execute SQL queries against Hive connections for data exploration and verification.

docs.title: Session Management
docs.text: Method Path Description POST /sessions Open a new data browser session DELETE /sessions/{sessionId} Close a session DELETE /sessions Close all sessions GET /sessions List active sessions GET /sessions/{sessionId}/status Check session status

docs.title: SQL Execution
docs.text: Method Path Description POST /sessions/{sessionId}/execute Execute a single SQL statement POST /sessions/{sessionId}/execute-script Execute a SQL script (multiple statements)

docs.title: Script Management
docs.text: Method Path Description GET /scripts List all saved scripts GET /scripts/{name}/exists Check if a script exists GET /scripts/{name} Load a script POST /scripts Save a script DELETE /scripts/{name} Delete a script

docs.title: Discovery
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/connections (shared with Connections API)

Method Path Description GET /{connectionKey}/databases List databases from a connection GET /{connectionKey}/databases/{databaseName}/tables List tables in a database

docs.title: Kerberos Credentials
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/kerberos-credentials

Method Path Description GET `` List all Kerberos credentials GET /{key} Get a Kerberos credential POST `` Create a Kerberos credential PUT /{key} Update a Kerberos credential DELETE /{key} Delete a Kerberos credential POST /{key}/test Test a Kerberos credential POST /upload-keytab Upload a keytab file POST /{key}/upload-keytab Upload a keytab for a specific credential GET /{key}/keytab-info Get keytab metadata and principals

docs.title: Certificates
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/certificates

Method Path Description GET /truststore/info Get truststore information GET `` List all certificates POST /upload Upload and import a certificate DELETE /{alias} Delete a certificate by alias POST /truststore/list List certificates in an uploaded truststore file POST /truststore/import Import selected certificates from a truststore POST /truststore/reset Reset truststore to Java default cacerts

docs.title: Driver Management
docs.text: Base Path: /api/v1/drivers

docs.title: Hive Drivers
docs.text: Method Path Description GET /available List available Hive drivers POST /download/{driverId} Download and install a Hive driver POST /refresh/{driverId} Refresh a Hive driver

docs.title: Trino Drivers
docs.text: Method Path Description GET /trino Get Trino driver information GET /trino/versions Get available Trino versions GET /trino/installed-versions Get installed Trino versions GET /trino/{version}/installed Check if a Trino version is installed POST /trino/download/{version} Download a Trino driver version POST /trino/refresh/{version} Refresh a Trino driver version

docs.title: Metastore Drivers
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/drivers/metastore

Method Path Description GET /available List available metastore drivers GET /{driverId}/versions List available versions for a driver GET /{driverId}/installed-versions List installed versions for a driver POST /download/{driverId}/{version} Download and install a driver version POST /refresh/{driverId}/{version} Refresh a driver version

docs.title: Preferences API
docs.text:
Base Path: /api/v1/preferences

The Preferences controller manages server-side configuration for Kerberos, JVM, TLS, LDAP, Backup, and Master Password settings. Changes to most settings require an application restart to take effect.

docs.title: Kerberos Preferences
docs.text: Method Path Description GET /kerberos Get Kerberos preferences POST /kerberos Save Kerberos preferences PUT /kerberos/settings Save Kerberos settings DELETE /kerberos Delete Kerberos preferences GET /kerberos/status Get Kerberos ticket status POST /kerberos/refresh Refresh Kerberos ticket GET /kerberos/configured Check if Kerberos is configured POST /kerberos/keytab Upload a keytab file POST /kerberos/keytab/principals List principals in a keytab

docs.title: JVM Preferences

docs.title: Embedded Ollama Management
docs.text:
Method Path Description GET /embedded/download Download Ollama binary (SSE streaming) POST /embedded/start Start embedded Ollama server POST /embedded/stop Stop embedded Ollama server

SSE Authentication

/embedded/download and /models/pull stream responses as text/event-stream. Because the browser EventSource API cannot set the Authorization header, pass the access token as a query parameter: ?token=<accessToken>. See Step 3 — Use the access token for SSE streams for details.

Import connections from legacy HMS-Mirror CLI configuration files.

Method Path Description GET /configs List available CLI config files GET /configs/{filename}/info Get connection info from a config file POST /connections Import a connection from a CLI config file

docs.title: Conditional Availability
docs.text:
Many API controllers are conditionally enabled based on feature flags and configuration properties:

Condition Controllers Affected hms-mirror.rocksdb.enabled=true Connections, Configurations, Datasets, Jobs, Runtime Jobs, Runtime Reports, Migration Plans, HCFS Profiles, CM Connector, Data Browser, Discovery, Kerberos Credentials, Trino Connections, Preferences, Application Preferences, LLM, Report Import, CLI Import hms-mirror.features.cm.enabled=true CM Connector (UI visibility) LLM provider configured LLM analysis and Advisor endpoints

Controllers not gated by RocksDB: Authentication, User Management, Certificates, Driver Management, Metastore Drivers, System Status, Application Info, Reports.

docs.title: cURL Examples
docs.text:
Authenticate: 

# Login and capture the access token
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"usernameOrEmail":"admin","password":"your-password"}' | jq -r '.accessToken')

List connections: 

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/connections

Create a job: 

curl -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/jobs/my-job \
  -d '{
    "name": "my-job",
    "dataStrategy": "SCHEMA_ONLY",
    "dataset": "my-dataset",
    "configuration": "my-config",
    "leftConnection": "source-cluster",
    "rightConnection": "target-cluster"
  }'

Start a job: 

curl -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/runtime/jobs/start \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"jobKey": "my-job", "dryRun": true}'

Check system status: 

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/system/status

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Swagger UI — Interactive API explorer with full schemas
  • CLI Reference — Command-line options
  • Configuration Reference — All application settings
  • Quick Start — Getting started guide

docs.title: Legacy CLI Retired
docs.text:
The standalone command-line interface from HMS-Mirror v3 has been retired in v4. The legacy CLI required complex YAML configuration files with deeply nested connection, migration, and optimization settings that were difficult to author and validate. Security credentials were stored in plain text configuration files, and the text-based reports lacked the interactive exploration capabilities needed for large-scale migrations.

HMS-Mirror v4 replaces the CLI with a Web UI and a comprehensive REST API:

  • Web UI — The recommended way to interact with HMS-Mirror. Provides visual connection testing, guided job configuration, interactive report exploration, and integrated documentation. All configuration is stored securely in RocksDB with encrypted credentials.
  • REST API — Full programmatic access to all HMS-Mirror functionality. Ideal for automation, CI/CD pipelines, and scripted migration workflows. See the API Reference for the complete endpoint catalog, or explore interactively via the Swagger UI.

Recommended Workflow

Use the Web UI to create and configure connections, datasets, configurations, and jobs — taking advantage of visual validation, connection testing, and guided wizards. Then use the REST API to automate job execution, integrate with scheduling systems, or build custom migration pipelines. View reports through the Web UI for the best experience, though report details are also available via the API.

docs.title: REST API Workflow Examples
docs.text: The examples below demonstrate a complete migration workflow using the REST API — from creating connections and datasets to running jobs and retrieving reports. This approach is ideal for automation and repeatable migration pipelines.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
All API calls require a JWT token. Obtain one first:

# Authenticate and extract the access token
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"your-password"}' | jq -r '.data.accessToken')

# Verify the token works
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/auth/me | jq .

docs.title: Step 1: Create Connections
docs.text:
Connections define how HMS-Mirror connects to your Hive clusters. We recommend creating and testing connections through the Web UI first, since it provides immediate visual feedback on connectivity issues, driver availability, and Kerberos configuration.

If you prefer the API:

# Create a source (left) connection
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/connections \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "hdp3-source",
    "description": "HDP 3.1 Source Cluster",
    "legacyHive": false,
    "hiveServer2": {
      "uri": "jdbc:hive2://hs2-source.example.com:10000/default",
      "connectionProperties": {
        "transportMode": "binary"
      }
    }
  }' | jq .

# Create a target (right) connection
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/connections \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "cdp-target",
    "description": "CDP 7.1 Target Cluster",
    "legacyHive": false,
    "hiveServer2": {
      "uri": "jdbc:hive2://hs2-target.example.com:10000/default",
      "connectionProperties": {
        "transportMode": "binary"
      }
    }
  }' | jq .

# Test the connections
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/connections/hdp3-source/test \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq .

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/connections/cdp-target/test \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq .

docs.title: Step 2: Create a Dataset
docs.text:
Datasets define which databases and tables to include in a migration. Each dataset specifies databases with optional include/exclude table patterns.

# Create a dataset targeting the sales and inventory databases
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/datasets \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "sales-migration",
    "description": "Sales and inventory databases for Q1 migration",
    "databases": [
      {
        "name": "sales_db",
        "tableIncludeFilterPattern": ".*",
        "tableExcludeFilterPattern": "tmp_.*"
      },
      {
        "name": "inventory_db",
        "tableIncludeFilterPattern": ".*"
      }
    ]
  }' | jq .

docs.title: Step 3: Create a Configuration
docs.text:
Configurations define migration behavior — ACID handling, optimizations, and other settings.

# Create a migration configuration
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/config \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "standard-migration",
    "description": "Standard migration with ACID downgrade",
    "migrateACID": {
      "on": true,
      "downgrade": true
    },
    "optimization": {
      "sortDynamicPartitionInserts": true
    },
    "copyViews": true
  }' | jq .

docs.title: Step 4: Create a Job Definition
docs.text:
Jobs bring together a dataset, configuration, and connections into an executable migration plan.

# Create a job
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/jobs/sales-q1-migration \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "sales-q1-migration",
    "description": "Migrate sales and inventory to CDP",
    "dataStrategy": "SCHEMA_ONLY",
    "dataset": "sales-migration",
    "configuration": "standard-migration",
    "leftConnection": "hdp3-source",
    "rightConnection": "cdp-target"
  }' | jq .

# Validate the job before running
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/jobs/sales-q1-migration/validate \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq .

docs.title: Step 5: Run the Job
docs.text:
Always start with a dry run to review the generated SQL before executing against your clusters.

# Start a dry run
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/runtime/jobs/start \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "jobKey": "sales-q1-migration",
    "dryRun": true
  }' | jq .

# Monitor job status
curl -s http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/runtime/jobs/current \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq .

# Once satisfied with the dry run, execute for real
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/runtime/jobs/start \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "jobKey": "sales-q1-migration",
    "dryRun": false
  }' | jq .

docs.title: Step 6: Retrieve Reports
docs.text:
Reports are best viewed through the Web UI, which provides interactive table-level detail, SQL previews, and downloadable artifacts. However, you can also retrieve report data through the API:

# List reports for the job
curl -s http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/jobs/sales-q1-migration/reports \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq .

# List all runtime reports
curl -s http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/runtime/reports \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq .

# Get detailed report information (use the key from the reports list)
REPORT_KEY="sales-q1-migration_20260210_143000"
curl -s "http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/runtime/reports/details?key=$REPORT_KEY" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq .

# Get database-level details with generated SQL
curl -s "http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/runtime/reports/database-details?key=$REPORT_KEY&database=sales_db" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq .

# List report files (SQL scripts, markdown reports)
curl -s "http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/runtime/reports/files?key=$REPORT_KEY" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq .

# Download a specific report file
curl -s "http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/runtime/reports/file?key=$REPORT_KEY&filename=RIGHT_execute.sql" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -o right_execute.sql

# Download all report artifacts as a zip
curl -s "http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/reports/download-by-key?key=$REPORT_KEY" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -o report.zip

docs.title: Putting It All Together: Automation Script
docs.text:
Here's a complete example script that automates the workflow:

#!/bin/bash
# HMS-Mirror REST API Migration Script
# Creates a dataset, builds a job, runs it, and downloads reports.

BASE_URL="http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1"
USERNAME="admin"
PASSWORD="your-password"

# Authenticate
echo "Authenticating..."
TOKEN=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/auth/login" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"username\":\"$USERNAME\",\"password\":\"$PASSWORD\"}" | jq -r '.data.accessToken')

if [ -z "$TOKEN" ] || [ "$TOKEN" = "null" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: Authentication failed"
  exit 1
fi

AUTH="-H \"Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN\""

# Create dataset
echo "Creating dataset..."
curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/datasets" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "nightly-migration",
    "databases": [{"name": "analytics_db", "tableIncludeFilterPattern": ".*"}]
  }'

# Create job
echo "Creating job..."
curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/jobs/nightly-run" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "nightly-run",
    "dataStrategy": "SCHEMA_ONLY",
    "dataset": "nightly-migration",
    "configuration": "standard-migration",
    "leftConnection": "hdp3-source",
    "rightConnection": "cdp-target"
  }'

# Validate
echo "Validating job..."
VALIDATION=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/jobs/nightly-run/validate" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN")
echo "$VALIDATION" | jq .

# Run dry run
echo "Starting dry run..."
curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/runtime/jobs/start" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"jobKey": "nightly-run", "dryRun": true}'

# Poll for completion
echo "Waiting for completion..."
while true; do
  STATUS=$(curl -sf "$BASE_URL/runtime/jobs/current" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN")
  RUNNING=$(echo "$STATUS" | jq -r '.data | length // 0')
  if [ "$RUNNING" = "0" ]; then
    echo "Job complete."
    break
  fi
  echo "  Still running..."
  sleep 5
done

# Download report
echo "Downloading reports..."
curl -sf "$BASE_URL/jobs/nightly-run/reports" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq .

echo "Done. View detailed reports at: http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror"

docs.title: Memory Settings
docs.text:
# Increase heap size
java -Xmx8g -Xms4g -jar hms-mirror-server.jar

# With G1 garbage collector
java -Xmx8g -XX:+UseG1GC -jar hms-mirror-server.jar

docs.title: Recommended Settings by Scale
docs.text: Scale Heap Options Small (<100 tables) 2G -Xmx2g Medium (<1000 tables) 4G -Xmx4g -XX:+UseG1GC Large (>1000 tables) 8G+ -Xmx8g -XX:+UseG1GC

docs.title: Debug Settings
docs.text:
# Enable remote debugging
java -Xdebug -Xrunjdwp:transport=dt_socket,server=y,suspend=n,address=5005 \
  -jar hms-mirror-server.jar

# Verbose GC logging
java -Xmx4g -verbose:gc -jar hms-mirror-server.jar

docs.title: External Configuration
docs.text:
# Use external application.properties
java -jar hms-mirror-server.jar \
  --spring.config.location=/etc/hms-mirror/application.properties

# Use external YAML
java -jar hms-mirror-server.jar \
  --spring.config.location=/etc/hms-mirror/application.yml

docs.title: Profile-Based Configuration
docs.text:
# Use production profile
java -jar hms-mirror-server.jar --spring.profiles.active=production

# Profile looks for application-production.yml

docs.title: Log Level
docs.text:
# Set log level
java -jar hms-mirror-server.jar \
  --logging.level.com.cloudera.utils.hms=DEBUG

# Multiple log levels
java -jar hms-mirror-server.jar \
  --logging.level.com.cloudera.utils.hms=DEBUG \
  --logging.level.org.apache.hive=WARN

docs.title: Log File
docs.text:
# Custom log file
java -jar hms-mirror-server.jar \
  --logging.file.name=/var/log/hms-mirror/app.log

docs.title: Log Levels
docs.text: Level Description ERROR Only errors WARN Warnings and errors INFO Normal operation (default) DEBUG Detailed debugging TRACE Maximum detail

docs.title: With Ticket Cache
docs.text:
# Obtain ticket first
kinit user@REALM

# Then start server
java -jar hms-mirror-server.jar

docs.title: With Keytab
docs.text:
java -Djava.security.auth.login.config=/path/to/jaas.conf \
  -jar hms-mirror-server.jar

Example jaas.conf: 

Client {
  com.sun.security.auth.module.Krb5LoginModule required
  useKeyTab=true
  keyTab="/path/to/keytab"
  principal="user@REALM";
};

docs.title: Kerberos Debug
docs.text:
java -Dsun.security.krb5.debug=true \
  -jar hms-mirror-server.jar

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Set options via environment variables:

export SERVER_PORT=8091
export HMS_MIRROR_ROCKSDB_PATH=/data/rocksdb
export HMS_MIRROR_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD=my-secure-password
export JAVA_OPTS="-Xmx8g -XX:+UseG1GC"

java $JAVA_OPTS -jar hms-mirror-server.jar

docs.title: Common Variables
docs.text: Variable Property Description SERVER_PORT server.port HTTP port HMS_MIRROR_ROCKSDB_PATH hms-mirror.rocksdb.path RocksDB location HMS_MIRROR_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD hms-mirror.config.keystore.password Keystore master password HMS_MIRROR_OUTPUT_DIR hms-mirror.config.output-dir Report output directory JAVA_OPTS — JVM options

docs.title: Using the Startup Script
docs.text: The distribution includes startup scripts:

docs.title: Linux/macOS
docs.text:
# Make executable (first time)
chmod +x bin/hms-mirror.sh

# Start server
./bin/hms-mirror.sh

# With options
./bin/hms-mirror.sh --server.port=8091

docs.title: Script Features
docs.text:
The startup script:

  • Sets appropriate JVM memory
  • Configures JAVA_HOME if needed
  • Adds aux_libs to classpath (for JDBC drivers)
  • Handles signals for clean shutdown

docs.title: Running as systemd Service
docs.text:
Create /etc/systemd/system/hms-mirror.service:

[Unit]
Description=HMS-Mirror Server
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=hms-mirror
Group=hms-mirror
WorkingDirectory=/opt/hms-mirror
ExecStart=/usr/bin/java -Xmx4g -jar /opt/hms-mirror/lib/hms-mirror-server.jar
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=10
Environment="JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17"
Environment="HMS_MIRROR_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD=your-production-password"

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

Commands: 

sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable hms-mirror
sudo systemctl start hms-mirror
sudo systemctl status hms-mirror
sudo systemctl stop hms-mirror

docs.title: View Service Logs
docs.text: sudo journalctl -u hms-mirror -f

docs.title: Running with Docker
docs.text:
# Basic run
docker run -d -p 8090:8090 \
  --name hms-mirror \
  cloudera/hms-mirror:4.x.x

# With persistent storage
docker run -d -p 8090:8090 \
  -v /path/to/data:/opt/hms-mirror/data \
  --name hms-mirror \
  cloudera/hms-mirror:4.x.x

# With environment variables
docker run -d -p 8090:8090 \
  -e SERVER_PORT=8090 \
  -e JAVA_OPTS="-Xmx4g" \
  -e HMS_MIRROR_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD="your-production-password" \
  --name hms-mirror \
  cloudera/hms-mirror:4.x.x

docs.title: Docker Compose
docs.text:
version: '3'
services:
  hms-mirror:
    image: cloudera/hms-mirror:4.x.x
    ports:
      - "8090:8090"
    volumes:
      - ./data:/opt/hms-mirror/data
      - ./config:/opt/hms-mirror/config
    environment:
      - JAVA_OPTS=-Xmx4g
      - HMS_MIRROR_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD=your-production-password
    restart: unless-stopped

docs.title: Development
docs.text:
# Quick start with defaults
java -jar hms-mirror-server.jar

# With debug logging
java -jar hms-mirror-server.jar \
  --logging.level.com.cloudera.utils.hms=DEBUG

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
# Different port to avoid conflicts
java -jar hms-mirror-server.jar --server.port=8091

# Separate RocksDB for testing
java -jar hms-mirror-server.jar \
  --hms-mirror.rocksdb.path=/tmp/hms-mirror-test/rocksdb

docs.title: Production
docs.text:
java -Xmx8g -XX:+UseG1GC \
  -jar hms-mirror-server.jar \
  --spring.profiles.active=production \
  --server.port=8090 \
  --hms-mirror.rocksdb.path=/data/hms-mirror/rocksdb \
  --logging.file.name=/var/log/hms-mirror/app.log

docs.title: Verify Server Running
docs.text:
# System health endpoint
curl http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/system/health

# Application version
curl http://localhost:8090/hms-mirror/api/v1/app/version

docs.title: Check Port
docs.text:
# Check if port is in use
lsof -i :8090
netstat -tlnp | grep 8090

docs.title: Graceful Shutdown
docs.text:
# Send SIGTERM
kill $(pgrep -f hms-mirror-server)

# Or via systemd
sudo systemctl stop hms-mirror

docs.title: Force Shutdown
docs.text:
# Send SIGKILL (last resort)
kill -9 $(pgrep -f hms-mirror-server)

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Meaning 0 Normal exit 1 General error 143 Terminated by signal

docs.title: Server Won't Start
docs.text:
# Check if port is in use
lsof -i :8090

# Check Java version (17+ required)
java -version

# Check RocksDB permissions
ls -la ~/.hms-mirror

docs.title: Out of Memory
docs.text:
# Increase heap
java -Xmx8g -jar hms-mirror-server.jar

docs.title: Permission Denied
docs.text:
# Check file permissions
chmod -R 755 ~/.hms-mirror

# Check user
whoami
ls -la ~/.hms-mirror
