docs.title: Configuration Reference
docs.text: This reference documents all HMS-Mirror configuration options.

docs.title: Application Properties
docs.text: Configure HMS-Mirror server settings in application.properties or application.yml.

docs.title: Server Configuration
docs.text:
Property Description Default server.port HTTP port 8090 server.servlet.context-path URL context path /hms-mirror 
server.port=8090
server.servlet.context-path=/hms-mirror

docs.title: RocksDB Storage
docs.text:
Property Description Default hms-mirror.rocksdb.enabled Enable RocksDB true hms-mirror.rocksdb.path Database location ~/.hms-mirror/rocksdb 
hms-mirror.rocksdb.enabled=true
hms-mirror.rocksdb.path=${user.home}/.hms-mirror/rocksdb

docs.title: Output Configuration
docs.text:
Property Description Default hms-mirror.config.output-dir Reports directory ~/.hms-mirror/reports hms-mirror.config.metastore-drivers-dir JDBC drivers ~/.hms-mirror/aux_libs 
hms-mirror.config.output-dir=${user.home}/.hms-mirror/reports
hms-mirror.config.metastore-drivers-dir=${user.home}/.hms-mirror/aux_libs

docs.title: Logging
docs.text:
Property Description Default logging.level.com.cloudera.utils.hms App log level INFO logging.file.name Log file path ~/.hms-mirror/logs/hms-mirror.log 
logging.level.com.cloudera.utils.hms=INFO
logging.file.name=${user.home}/.hms-mirror/logs/hms-mirror.log

docs.title: HiveServer2 Settings
docs.text:
Property Type Description uri String JDBC connection URI driverClassName String JDBC driver class connectionProperties Map Connection properties sessionProperties Map Hive session properties 
hiveServer2:
  uri: jdbc:hive2://hs2.example.com:10000/default
  driverClassName: org.apache.hive.jdbc.HiveDriver
  connectionProperties:
    transportMode: binary
    ssl: "true"
  sessionProperties:
    hive.exec.parallel: "true"

docs.title: Metastore Direct Settings
docs.text:
Property Type Description type Enum Database type (MYSQL, POSTGRESQL, ORACLE) uri String JDBC connection URI username String Database username password String Database password 
metastoreDirect:
  type: MYSQL
  uri: jdbc:mysql://metastore.example.com:3306/hive
  username: hive_user
  password: password

docs.title: Partition Discovery
docs.text:
Property Type Description Default auto Boolean Auto-discover partitions true 
partitionDiscovery:
  auto: true

docs.title: Details Section
docs.text: Property Type Description name String Configuration name description String Configuration description

docs.title: Migration Section
docs.text:
Property Type Description Default copyViews Boolean Migrate view definitions false viewsOnly Boolean Only process views false evaluatePartitionLocation Boolean Check partition locations false tablePropertyFilter String Regex to filter properties null resetToDefaultLocation Boolean Reset table locations false 
migration:
  copyViews: true
  viewsOnly: false
  evaluatePartitionLocation: true
  tablePropertyFilter: "^(?!TRANSLATED).*"
  resetToDefaultLocation: false

docs.title: Optimizations Section
docs.text:
Property Type Description Default sortDynamicPartitionInserts Boolean Sort before partition insert false autoTune Boolean Auto-optimize settings false textCompression Boolean Enable text compression false compressionCodec String Compression codec null buildShadowStatistics Boolean Build stats during migration false overrides List Property overrides [] 
optimizations:
  sortDynamicPartitionInserts: true
  autoTune: true
  textCompression: true
  compressionCodec: ZSTD
  buildShadowStatistics: true
  overrides:
    - key: hive.exec.parallel
      leftValue: "true"
      rightValue: "true"

docs.title: Transfer Section
docs.text:
Property Type Description warehouse.external String External table warehouse warehouse.managed String Managed table warehouse commonStorage String Shared storage path intermediateStorage String Temp storage for export globalLocationMap Map Namespace translations 
transfer:
  warehouse:
    external: s3a://bucket/warehouse/external
    managed: s3a://bucket/warehouse/managed
  commonStorage: s3a://shared/common
  intermediateStorage: hdfs:///tmp/hms-mirror
  globalLocationMap:
    "hdfs://old-ns": "s3a://new-bucket"

docs.title: Storage Migration Section
docs.text:
Property Type Description Default translationType Enum RELATIVE or ALIGNED RELATIVE dataMovementStrategy Enum SQL, DISTCP, EXPORT_IMPORT, MANUAL SQL dataFlow Enum PULL or PUSH PULL 
transfer:
  storageMigration:
    translationType: ALIGNED
    dataMovementStrategy: DISTCP
    dataFlow: PULL

docs.title: ACID Section
docs.text:
Property Type Description Default includeAcidTables Boolean Process ACID tables false acidOnly Boolean Only process ACID tables false downgradeAcid Boolean Convert to external false inPlace Boolean In-place migration false migrateAcid Boolean Full ACID migration false partitionLimit Integer Max partitions 0 artificialBucketThreshold Integer Min buckets for SQL 0 
acid:
  includeAcidTables: true
  acidOnly: false
  downgradeAcid: true
  inPlace: false
  migrateAcid: false
  partitionLimit: 500
  artificialBucketThreshold: 2

docs.title: Iceberg Section
docs.text:
Property Type Description Default enabled Boolean Enable Iceberg conversion false conversionMode Enum IN_PLACE or SHADOW SHADOW version Integer Iceberg format version 2 fileFormat Enum PARQUET, ORC, AVRO PARQUET tableProperties Map Iceberg table properties {} 
iceberg:
  enabled: true
  conversionMode: SHADOW
  version: 2
  fileFormat: PARQUET
  tableProperties:
    write.format.default: parquet
    write.parquet.compression-codec: zstd

docs.title: Dataset Configuration
docs.text: Property Type Description name String Dataset name description String Dataset description databases List Database definitions

docs.title: Database Definition
docs.text:
Property Type Description name String Source database name includePattern String Regex for included tables excludePattern String Regex for excluded tables targetName String Target database name targetWarehouse String Custom warehouse location 
name: sales-dataset
description: Sales database tables
databases:
  - name: sales_db
    includePattern: "fact_.*|dim_.*"
    excludePattern: "test_.*"
    targetName: sales_prod_db
    targetWarehouse: s3a://sales/warehouse

docs.title: Job Configuration
docs.text: Property Type Description name String Job name description String Job description dataStrategy Enum Migration strategy dataset String Dataset reference configuration String Configuration reference leftConnection String Source connection rightConnection String Target connection

docs.title: Data Strategy Values
docs.text: Value Description SCHEMA_ONLY Schema without data LINKED Create linked tables SQL Move data via SQL EXPORT_IMPORT Use Hive export/import HYBRID Automatic strategy selection STORAGE_MIGRATION Same-cluster storage move COMMON Shared storage DUMP DDL extraction only ICEBERG_CONVERSION Convert to Iceberg

docs.title: Job Options
docs.text:
Property Type Description resetToDefaultLocation Boolean Reset table locations globalLocationMap Map Namespace mappings sqlPartitionLimit Integer Max partitions for SQL exportPartitionLimit Integer Max partitions for export 
name: migration-job
description: Migrate sales to CDP
dataStrategy: HYBRID
dataset: sales-dataset
configuration: migration-config
leftConnection: hdp-cluster
rightConnection: cdp-cluster
options:
  resetToDefaultLocation: false
  sqlPartitionLimit: 100
  exportPartitionLimit: 500

docs.title: LLM Configuration
docs.text: Property Type Description Default enabled Boolean Enable LLM features false provider Enum LLM provider EMBEDDED_OLLAMA model String Model name llama3.1 temperature Float Creativity (0-1) 0.7 maxTokens Integer Max response tokens 4096 endpoint String API endpoint (for remote) null apiKey String API key (for cloud) null

docs.title: Provider Values
docs.text:
Value Description EMBEDDED_OLLAMA Built-in Ollama LOCAL_OLLAMA External Ollama OPENAI OpenAI API ANTHROPIC Anthropic API AZURE_OPENAI Azure OpenAI AWS_BEDROCK AWS Bedrock GOOGLE_AI Google AI 
llm:
  enabled: true
  provider: LOCAL_OLLAMA
  model: llama3.1
  temperature: 0.7
  maxTokens: 4096
  endpoint: http://localhost:11434

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Override properties with environment variables:

Property Environment Variable server.port SERVER_PORT hms-mirror.rocksdb.path HMS_MIRROR_ROCKSDB_PATH hms-mirror.config.output-dir HMS_MIRROR_OUTPUT_DIR 
export SERVER_PORT=8091
export HMS_MIRROR_ROCKSDB_PATH=/data/hms-mirror/rocksdb
java -jar hms-mirror-server.jar

docs.title: Complete Configuration Example
docs.text:
# application.yml

server:
  port: 8090
  servlet:
    context-path: /hms-mirror

hms-mirror:
  rocksdb:
    enabled: true
    path: ${user.home}/.hms-mirror/rocksdb
  config:
    output-dir: ${user.home}/.hms-mirror/reports
    metastore-drivers-dir: ${user.home}/.hms-mirror/aux_libs

logging:
  level:
    com.cloudera.utils.hms: INFO
  file:
    name: ${user.home}/.hms-mirror/logs/hms-mirror.log

spring:
  profiles:
    active: production

docs.title: Server Defaults
docs.text: Setting Default Port 8090 Context path /hms-mirror RocksDB enabled true RocksDB path ~/.hms-mirror/rocksdb

docs.title: Migration Defaults
docs.text: Setting Default Copy views false Evaluate partition location false Sort dynamic partition inserts false Auto tune false

docs.title: ACID Defaults
docs.text: Setting Default Include ACID tables false Downgrade ACID false Partition limit 0 (unlimited) Bucket threshold 0

docs.title: Iceberg Defaults
docs.text: Setting Default Enabled false Conversion mode SHADOW Version 2 File format PARQUET

docs.text:

  • API Reference - REST API documentation
  • Installation - Setup instructions

docs.title: Data Browser
docs.text: The Data Browser allows you to execute SQL queries directly against your Hive connections from within HMS-Mirror. This is useful for exploring databases, verifying table structures, and testing queries before running migrations.

docs.title: Accessing the Data Browser
docs.text: Navigate to Utilities > Data Browser from the main menu.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Before using the Data Browser, you must have at least one connection configured with a successful HiveServer2 test. Only connections that have passed the HS2 connection test will appear in the connection selector.

docs.title: Opening a Connection Tab
docs.text:
1. Select a connection from the dropdown menu at the top of the page
  2. Click New Tab to open a session to that connection
  3. Each tab represents an independent session to the selected cluster

You can open multiple tabs to different connections simultaneously, allowing you to compare data across clusters.

docs.title: Writing SQL
docs.text:
The SQL editor supports standard HiveQL syntax. Key features include:

  • Comments: Lines starting with -- are treated as comments and ignored during execution
  • Inline comments: Text after -- on any line is ignored (unless inside a quoted string)
  • Statement termination: Statements must end with a semicolon (;)
  • Multi-line statements: Statements can span multiple lines

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
-- Show all databases
SHOW DATABASES;

-- List tables in a specific database
USE my_database;
SHOW TABLES;

-- Query with inline comment
SELECT * FROM my_table LIMIT 10; -- preview data

docs.title: Run Current Statement
docs.text:
Executes the statement at the current cursor position.

  • Keyboard shortcut: ⌘+Enter (Mac) or Ctrl+Enter (Windows/Linux)
  • Button: Click Run Statement

The statement is determined by the cursor position: - If the cursor is within a statement, that statement is executed - If the cursor is on the same line as a semicolon (even after it), the statement ending with that semicolon is executed

docs.title: Run Script
docs.text:
Executes all statements in the editor sequentially.

  • Keyboard shortcut: ⌘+Shift+Enter (Mac) or Ctrl+Shift+Enter (Windows/Linux)
  • Button: Click Run Script

Execution stops if any statement encounters an error.

docs.title: Viewing Results
docs.text:
Query results appear in tabbed panels below the SQL editor:

  • Each executed query creates a new result tab
  • Result tabs show a success/error indicator
  • Query results display the first 100 rows in a scrollable table
  • Column names and data types are shown in the table header
  • NULL values are displayed as italicized "NULL"

docs.title: Result Types
docs.text:
• Query Results: SELECT statements show data in a table format with row count
  • Update Results: DDL and DML statements show a success message with rows affected
  • Errors: Failed statements display the error message and SQL state

docs.title: Saving and Loading Scripts
docs.text: The Data Browser allows you to save scripts for later use and reload them when needed.

docs.title: Saving a Script
docs.text:
1. Click the Save button in the toolbar
  2. Enter a name for your script
  3. Click Save

If a script with that name already exists, you will be prompted to confirm overwriting. You can check "Always overwrite without asking" to skip this confirmation for future saves during your session.

docs.title: Loading a Script
docs.text:
1. Click the Load button in the toolbar
  2. Select a script from the list of saved scripts
  3. The script content will replace the current editor content

You can also delete saved scripts by hovering over a script name and clicking the trash icon.

Scripts are stored persistently and will be available across sessions.

docs.title: Closing Sessions
docs.text: Click the X on any tab to close that session. This releases the connection back to the pool. Closing all tabs returns you to the initial state where you can select a new connection.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Use SHOW DATABASES and SHOW TABLES to explore the cluster
  • Use DESCRIBE FORMATTED table_name to see detailed table metadata
  • Limit large queries with LIMIT to avoid long-running operations
  • The browser maintains separate sessions per tab, so transactions are isolated

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
docs.text: Common questions about HMS-Mirror v4.

docs.title: What is HMS-Mirror?
docs.text: HMS-Mirror is a tool for migrating and synchronizing Apache Hive metadata and data between DataLake environments powered by Hive and Hive Metastore. It automates the complex process of moving tables, views, and data between environments like HDP to CDP, on-premises to cloud, or HDFS to object storage.

docs.title: What's new in version 4?
docs.text: Version 4 introduces: - Web-based UI - Visual configuration instead of config files - Wizard-driven workflows - Step-by-step guidance - AI-powered analysis - LLM integration for insights - RocksDB storage - Persistent configurations without external database - Improved job management - Save, validate, and rerun jobs - Iceberg conversion - Convert tables to Iceberg format

docs.title: Is HMS-Mirror open source?
docs.text: Yes, HMS-Mirror is open source software developed by Cloudera Labs.

docs.title: What clusters does HMS-Mirror support?
docs.text:
Source clusters (LEFT): - HDP 2.x and 3.x - CDH 5.x and 6.x - CDP Private Cloud Base - CDP Public Cloud

Target clusters (RIGHT): - CDP Private Cloud Base - CDP Public Cloud (AWS, Azure, GCP) - Open-source Hive deployments

docs.title: Does HMS-Mirror require downtime?
docs.text: No mandatory downtime, but consider: - For SCHEMA_ONLY: No impact on source - For data migration: Source tables are read (may affect performance) - For STORAGE_MIGRATION: Brief table unavailability possible - Best practice: Run during low-activity periods for large migrations

docs.title: What are the system requirements?
docs.text: Requirement Minimum Recommended Java JDK 11 JDK 17 Memory 2 GB 4 GB+ Disk 500 MB 2 GB+

docs.title: Do I need to install anything on the clusters?
docs.text: No. HMS-Mirror connects to clusters via standard JDBC (HiveServer2) or direct Metastore connections. No agents or software installation on clusters is required.

docs.title: Can I run HMS-Mirror in Docker?
docs.text:
Yes. Docker images are available: 

docker run -d -p 8090:8090 cloudera/hms-mirror:4.x.x

docs.title: Where does HMS-Mirror store data?
docs.text: By default in ~/.hms-mirror/: - rocksdb/ - Configurations, jobs, datasets - reports/ - Migration reports - aux_libs/ - JDBC drivers - logs/ - Application logs

docs.title: What authentication methods are supported?
docs.text:
• No authentication
  • Kerberos
  • LDAP (username/password)
  • Knox Gateway

docs.title: Do I need both HiveServer2 and Metastore Direct?
docs.text:
HiveServer2 is required for most operations (creating tables, moving data).

Metastore Direct is optional but provides: - Faster metadata queries - Direct database access when HS2 unavailable

docs.title: How do I connect through Knox?
docs.text:
Use the Knox gateway URI: 

jdbc:hive2://knox.example.com:8443/;ssl=true;transportMode=http;httpPath=gateway/cdp-proxy-api/hive

docs.title: Can I connect to clusters in different Kerberos realms?
docs.text: Yes, with cross-realm trust configured. Configure both realms in /etc/krb5.conf with appropriate trust relationships.

docs.title: Which strategy should I use?
docs.text: Scenario Recommended Schema sync only SCHEMA_ONLY Quick test access LINKED Small tables (<1GB) SQL Large tables (>1GB) EXPORT_IMPORT Mixed table sizes HYBRID Same cluster, new storage STORAGE_MIGRATION Shared storage COMMON DDL documentation DUMP Table format upgrade ICEBERG_CONVERSION

docs.title: What's the difference between SQL and EXPORT_IMPORT?
docs.text:
SQL Strategy - Uses INSERT...SELECT through HiveServer2 - Data is serialized/deserialized - Good for small-medium tables - Can apply transformations

EXPORT_IMPORT Strategy - Uses Hive's native export/import - Preserves exact data formats - Better for large tables - Required for ACID tables (without downgrade)

docs.title: How does HYBRID work?
docs.text: HYBRID automatically selects SQL or EXPORT_IMPORT per table based on: - Table size - Partition count - Whether it's ACID - Bucket configuration

docs.title: Can I migrate ACID tables?
docs.text: Yes, with options: - Keep ACID: Use EXPORT_IMPORT strategy - Downgrade: Convert to external tables (easier, recommended for legacy migrations)

docs.title: Does HMS-Mirror actually move data?
docs.text: Depends on strategy: - SCHEMA_ONLY, LINKED, DUMP, COMMON: No data movement - SQL, EXPORT_IMPORT, HYBRID: Yes, moves data - STORAGE_MIGRATION: Moves data within cluster

docs.title: How long does migration take?
docs.text:
Varies greatly based on: - Data volume - Number of tables - Strategy used - Network bandwidth - Cluster resources

Rough estimates (per table): - Schema only: Seconds - Small table (SQL): Minutes - Large table (EXPORT_IMPORT): Minutes to hours

docs.title: Can I run migrations in parallel?
docs.text: HMS-Mirror processes tables within a database sequentially but can process multiple operations concurrently. For maximum parallelism, split into multiple jobs by database.

docs.title: What if a migration fails midway?
docs.text:
• Failed tables are noted in the report
  • Successful tables are not affected
  • Create a new job with only failed tables
  • Fix the issue and re-run

docs.title: Why are ACID tables special?
docs.text: ACID (transactional) tables have: - Different storage format - Version compatibility issues between Hive versions - Restrictions on SQL operations

docs.title: Should I downgrade ACID tables?
docs.text:
Yes, if: - Migrating from HDP to CDP - Applications don't need UPDATE/DELETE - Simpler management preferred

No, if: - Applications actively use transactions - UPDATE/DELETE operations required - Compliance requires transactional guarantees

docs.title: What happens when ACID tables are downgraded?
docs.text:
• Converted to external tables
  • Lose transactional properties
  • Data is preserved
  • Can't use UPDATE/DELETE after

docs.title: Can I migrate from HDFS to S3?
docs.text:
Yes, using STORAGE_MIGRATION strategy: 

dataStrategy: STORAGE_MIGRATION
transfer:
  warehouse:
    external: s3a://bucket/warehouse/external
    managed: s3a://bucket/warehouse/managed

docs.title: Do I need to update table locations manually?
docs.text: No. HMS-Mirror updates all table and partition locations automatically based on your configuration.

docs.title: What about credentials for cloud storage?
docs.text: Configure in core-site.xml or pass through Hive session properties. HMS-Mirror uses the cluster's configured storage credentials.

docs.title: What is Iceberg?
docs.text: Apache Iceberg is a high-performance table format providing: - Time travel queries - Schema evolution - Hidden partitioning - Multi-engine support

docs.title: Can I convert existing tables to Iceberg?
docs.text: Yes, using ICEBERG_CONVERSION strategy. Supports in-place or shadow conversion.

docs.title: Do I lose data during Iceberg conversion?
docs.text: No. Data is preserved. With SHADOW mode, the original table is also retained.

docs.title: Can I use HMS-Mirror without the web UI?
docs.text: The web UI is the primary interface in v4. The API is available for automation, but there's no standalone CLI mode like v3.

docs.title: How do I back up my configurations?
docs.text:
1. Export individual items from the UI
  2. Back up the RocksDB directory: ~/.hms-mirror/rocksdb

docs.title: Can multiple users access HMS-Mirror simultaneously?
docs.text: Yes, but there's no user authentication or multi-tenancy. All users share the same configurations and jobs.

docs.title: Do I need LLM features?
docs.text:
No. LLM features are optional and provide: - Configuration analysis - Job recommendations - Report insights

All core functionality works without LLM.

docs.title: Is my data sent to cloud AI services?
docs.text: Depends on configuration: - Embedded/Local Ollama: All processing local, no data sent externally - Cloud providers (OpenAI, etc.): Data sent to provider APIs

docs.title: What models are recommended?
docs.text: Use Case Model Quick analysis mistral, phi Detailed analysis llama3.1, gpt-4 Privacy-focused Embedded Ollama

docs.title: Why can't I connect to my cluster?
docs.text: Common causes: 1. Network/firewall blocking connection 2. Wrong hostname or port 3. Service not running 4. Authentication failure (wrong credentials, no Kerberos ticket)

docs.title: Why is my migration slow?
docs.text: Possible causes: 1. Large data volumes 2. Using SQL for large tables (use EXPORT_IMPORT) 3. Network bandwidth limitations 4. Cluster resource constraints

docs.title: How do I enable debug logging?
docs.text:
java -jar hms-mirror-server.jar \
  --logging.level.com.cloudera.utils.hms=DEBUG

docs.title: Where are the logs?
docs.text: Default: ~/.hms-mirror/logs/hms-mirror.log

docs.title: How should I plan a large migration?
docs.text:
1. Inventory: Catalog all databases and tables
  2. Classify: Identify ACID, large tables, complex cases
  3. Test: Start with small, non-critical databases
  4. Validate: Verify results thoroughly
  5. Scale: Gradually migrate larger datasets
  6. Cutover: Plan application switchover

docs.title: Should I migrate everything at once?
docs.text: No. Recommended approach: 1. Phase 1: Schema only (validate structure) 2. Phase 2: Small tables 3. Phase 3: Large tables 4. Phase 4: Complex cases (ACID, many partitions)

docs.title: How do I validate a migration?
docs.text:
• Compare row counts
  • Run sample queries on both clusters
  • Check table properties
  • Verify partition locations
  • Test application access

docs.title: Where can I get support?
docs.text:
• Documentation (this guide)
  • GitHub Issues for bugs/features
  • Community forums

docs.title: How do I report a bug?
docs.text: Open an issue on GitHub with: - HMS-Mirror version - Steps to reproduce - Expected vs actual behavior - Logs (with sensitive info removed)
